title_original: US-Israeli war on Iran expands and shuts down Gulf oil and gas production
content_original: US-Israeli war on Iran expands and shuts down Gulf oil and gas production Submitted by Adam Chamseddine on Mon, 03/02/2026 - 16:33 Hezbollah firing on Israel sparks Lebanon bombing campaign while rockets fired at British base in Cyprus A woman with her child looks at the aftermath of an Israeli and US strike on a police station in Tehran, Iran, 2 March 2026 (Majid Asgaripour/WANA via Reuters) On The US - Israeli conflict with Iran escalated across the region on Monday, with  Iranian strikes prompting  Saudi Arabia and Qatar to halt production at major oil and gas facilities, and bombing spreading to  Lebanon  too. Meanwhile, three US F-15s were downed in Kuwait, Qatar shot down two fighter jets approaching from Iran, and rockets were launched at a British military base in Cyprus. US and Israeli bombing on Iran continued apace for a third day, with President Donald Trump warning the "big wave" is yet to come. Tehran's response has targeted Israel and US assets across...
title_en: US-Israeli war on Iran expands and shuts down Gulf oil and gas production
summary_en: The US-Israel conflict with Iran has escalated significantly, with Iranian strikes prompting Saudi Arabia and Qatar to halt production at major oil and gas facilities. The conflict has expanded geographically with bombing spreading to Lebanon, three US F-15s downed in Kuwait, and rockets fired at a British base in Cyprus, marking a major regional escalation affecting critical energy infrastructure and multiple US military assets.

classification.location: Middle East - Gulf region, Lebanon, Kuwait
classification.brief: Major escalation of US-Iran conflict with Iranian strikes causing Gulf oil/gas production shutdown, F-15s downed in Kuwait, and conflict spreading to Lebanon.

title_original: Hezbollah said to have launched drone that struck UK RAF airbase in Cyprus
content_original: Two more drones intercepted on Monday, authorities say, in what appears to be sustained targeting of base US-Israel war on Iran – live updates A one-way attack drone – said to have been launched by Lebanon’s Hezbollah – struck the UK’s RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus at about midnight on Sunday, prompting a partial evacuation of the military facility. Two more drones were successfully intercepted on Monday morning, the Cypriot authorities said, as part of what appears to be a sustained targeting of the base on the third day of the war in the Middle East. Continue reading...
title_en: Hezbollah said to have launched drone that struck UK RAF airbase in Cyprus
summary_en: Hezbollah launched a one-way attack drone that struck the UK's RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus at about midnight on Sunday, prompting a partial evacuation of the military facility. Two more drones were successfully intercepted on Monday morning by Cypriot authorities, marking the third consecutive day of sustained targeting of the base as part of the expanding Middle East conflict.

classification.brief: Hezbollah launched a one-way attack drone striking the UK's RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus, with two additional drones intercepted by Cypriot authorities on the third consecutive day of attacks.

content_original: אחד האתגרים המרכזיים של מערכות ההגנה של צה"ל מול הטילים האיראנים הוא הטיל בעל ראש נפץ מתפרק אשר מקשה על מערכות ההגנה ליירט אותו. במהלך היממה האחרונה, כחלק ממבצע "שאגת הארי", שבו האיראנים ושיגרו את החימוש המורכב הזה, בדיוק כפי שעשו בעבר במהלך מבצע "עם כלביא". מדובר בטקטיקה שנועדה לייצר מציאות שונה בשמיים, כאשר בניגוד לטיל סטנדרטי הנושא ראש קרב אחד גדול, כאן המערכת מתמודדת עם פיצול שמייצר "ענן" של איומים קטנים בו-זמנית. כך פועל הטיל המתפרק שמאתגר את מערכות ההגנה 1 מדובר בטיל שמשחרר "פצצונות" – ראשי נפץ קטנים במשקל של 3-2 ק"ג כל אחד – בזמן שהוא בשמיים והן מתפזרות על שטח נרחב. המנגנון פועל בצורה מדויקת: כשהטיל מגיע לגובה מסוים (גובה של כ-7 ק"מ מעל הקרקע), ראש הנפץ המרכזי "נפתח". מתוך הראש משתחררות עשרות פצצות קטנות יותר (תתי-חימושים). במקרה שדווח, כל פצצה שקלה בין 2.5 ל-2.7 ק"ג. התוצאה היא שטח כיסוי נרחב במיוחד, כאשר הפצצות מתפזרות ברדיוס רחב (דווח על רדיוס של כ-8 ק"מ) ונוחתות על הקרקע, שם הן מתפוצצות במ...
title_en: How the fragmentation missile that challenges defense systems works
summary_en: One of the central challenges for Israel's defense systems against Iranian missiles is the fragmentation warhead missile, which makes it difficult for defense systems to intercept. During the past day, as part of 'Roar of the Lion' operation, Iranians launched this complex weapon, similar to what they did in 'True Promise' operation. The tactic is designed to create a different reality in the skies - unlike a standard missile carrying one large warhead, here the system faces a splitting that produces a 'cloud' of small threats simultaneously. The missile releases 'bomblets' - small warheads weighing 2-3 kg each - while in the air, spreading over a wide area.

classification.brief: Iranian forces launched fragmentation warhead missiles during the 'Roar of the Lion' operation, challenging Israeli defense systems.

title_original: Bahrain detains people allegedly celebrating Iran strikes

Long seen as havens of peace and stability in the region, the Gulf Arab states have come under attack by Iran  after a US-Israel offensive started on Saturday, which has killed hundreds of Iranians, including dozens of senior officials, such as Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei . Tehran claims it is targeting US interests in the Gulf, but it has also hit civilian infrastructure in Gulf states, including airports and hotels. title_original: Oil tanker attacked off coast of US ally (VIDEO)
content_original: The vessel is the third to be targeted in the region since Washington and Israel launched strikes on Iran A crew member has been killed in an attack on an oil tanker off the coast of Oman, local media reported on Monday, citing the Defense Ministry. The vessel is the third to be targeted in the region since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran. Massive bombing strikes on Iran began on Saturday, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, senior officials, and hundreds of civilians. Iran has responded with missile and drone attacks on Israel and US bases, while the escalation has disrupted shipping in the crucial Strait of Hormuz passage. The Marshall Islands-flagged oil tanker MKD VYOM was attacked by an explosive-laden remotely operated boat while it was sailing 52 nautical miles (over 95 km) off the coast of Muscat Governorate, according to Omani authorities. The attack resulted in a fire and explosion in the main engine room, with one fatality and the remaining 21 crew evac...

The attack further escalates tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping chokepoint, amid Iran's ongoing missile and drone retaliation against Israel and US bases.

classification.brief: Iranian proxy forces attacked a Marshall Islands-flagged oil tanker off Oman, killing one crew member and escalating tensions in the critical Strait of Hormuz shipping lane as part of Iran's retaliatory campaign following US and Israel strikes on Iran.

title_en: Lost husband and mother-in-law in Bet Shemesh tragedy: 'On the bar mitzvah day, the son buried his father'
summary_en: An Iranian missile struck a shelter in Bet Shemesh, Israel, killing nine people including Pnina Cohen's husband Yossi and mother-in-law Bruriah. Cohen was in the shelter with her family during the strike and was seriously injured, requiring surgery at Ein Karem hospital; she has not been able to see her son since the attack. This incident is part of Iran's ongoing ballistic missile campaign against Israel as part of the broader Iran-US armed conflict.

classification.brief: Iranian ballistic missile struck a shelter in Bet Shemesh, Israel, killing nine people including a husband and mother-in-law.

title_original: Israeli army issues expulsion orders for 18 Lebanese towns
content_original: Israeli army issues expulsion orders for 18 Lebanese towns The Israeli army has issued expulsion orders for 18 Lebanese villages and towns, claiming they are being used by Hezbollah. These include locations in the Nabatieh area, Tyre district and the Beqaa valley. The move comes after Israel bombarded Beirut’s southern suburbs, following Hezbollah’s overnight attacks on Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Health officials reported that Israeli attacks have killed over 30 people and wounded 149 more in Lebanon since the resumption of hostilities.
title_en: Israeli army issues expulsion orders for 18 Lebanese towns
summary_en: The Israeli army has issued expulsion orders for 18 Lebanese villages and towns in the Nabatieh area, Tyre district, and Beqaa valley, claiming these locations are being used by Hezbollah. This comes amid intensified Israeli bombardment of Beirut's southern suburbs following Hezbollah's retaliatory attacks for the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader. Over 30 people have been killed and 149 wounded in Lebanon since the resumption of hostilities, demonstrating the expanding multi-front nature of the conflict beyond direct Iran-US exchanges.

classification.location: Nabatieh, Tyre district, Beqaa valley, Lebanon
classification.brief: Israeli army issues expulsion orders for 18 Lebanese villages and towns in Nabatieh, Tyre and Beqaa areas, claiming Hezbollah presence, amid intensified bombardment of Beirut's southern suburbs following Hezbollah retaliation.

This represents a significant escalation in the ongoing Iran-US conflict, involving massive coordinated air and missile operations against Iranian military infrastructure, IRGC assets, and possibly nuclear facilities. The scale of the strikes indicates a major offensive campaign aimed at degrading Iran's military capabilities.

classification.brief: US and Israel conducted coordinated strikes on approximately 2,000 targets inside Iran, marking a significant escalation in the Iran-US conflict.

title_en: Lebanon furious at Hezbollah joining the campaign: 'It will bring us more destruction, we will pay the price'
summary_en: Lebanese citizens and officials are criticizing Hezbollah for launching rockets toward Israel, fearing it will bring further destruction to Lebanon. Lebanese journalists and political figures accused Hezbollah of dragging the country into war and called on the group to bear the consequences alone, while expressing concerns about mass displacement and escalation following the attacks.

classification.brief: Lebanese citizens and officials criticize Hezbollah for launching rockets toward Israel, warning it will bring further destruction to Lebanon and drag the country into war.

title_original: Joint Statement on Iran's Missile and Drone Attacks in the Region
content_original: Doha, March 02 (QNA) - The State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Kingdom of Bahrain, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the State of Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates, and the United States of America strongly condemn the Islamic Republic of Iran's indiscriminate and reckless missile and drone attacks against sovereign territories across the region, including Bahrain, Iraq -including the Iraqi Kurdistan Region- Jordan, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates.
title_en: Joint Statement on Iran's Missile and Drone Attacks in the Region
summary_en: Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, UAE, and the United States jointly condemned Iran's indiscriminate missile and drone attacks against sovereign territories across the region, including Bahrain, Iraq (including Iraqi Kurdistan Region), Jordan, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE. The coordinated statement from both Gulf states and the US demonstrates broader regional alignment against Iranian aggression and represents significant escalation in cross-border hostilities targeting multiple US-aligned Gulf partners.

classification.location: Gulf region (Bahrain, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia)
classification.brief: Iran conducted indiscriminate missile and drone attacks against multiple Gulf states, prompting joint condemnation from the US and Arab allies.

title_original: Qatar downs Iran warplanes, halts LNG production as Gulf crisis deepens
content_original: Qatar shot down two Iranian fighter jets and halted LNG production over Iran's latest strikes, sending prices soaring, as the Gulf crisis escalated sharply on Monday. Qatar's air force downed two Sukhoi SU24 bombers, the defence ministry said -- the first such action by a Gulf country after three days of Iranian bombardment. The wealthy region of staunch US allies has found itself in the firing line of Iran's retaliatory attacks, with airports and hotels hit along with military sites. Six people have been killed and dozens injured.
title_en: Qatar downs Iran warplanes, halts LNG production as Gulf crisis deepens
summary_en: Qatar's air force shot down two Iranian Sukhoi SU-24 bombers that were conducting strikes against Gulf targets, marking the first direct intervention by a Gulf country in the ongoing Iran-US conflict. Qatar also halted LNG production as the conflict escalated, causing energy prices to surge. Six people were killed and dozens injured in Iran's bombardment of airports, hotels, and military sites across the Gulf region.

classification.brief: Qatar's air force shot down two Iranian SU-24 bombers conducting strikes against Gulf targets, marking the first direct Gulf country intervention in the Iran-US conflict and halting LNG production.

The service member had been seriously wounded during Iran’s initial attacks and eventually succumbed to their injuries, it said in a statement. CENTCOM Update TAMPA, Fla. – As of 7:30 am ET, March 2, four U.S. service members have been killed in action. The fourth service member, who was seriously wounded during Iran’s initial attacks, eventually succumbed to their injuries. Major combat operations continue and our… — U.S. Central Command (@CENTCOM) March 2, 2026

summary_en: A fourth US service member has died of injuries sustained during the US operation in Iran. The service member was seriously wounded during Iran's initial ballistic missile and drone attacks and eventually succumbed to their injuries. CENTCOM confirmed the death as of March 2, 2026, with major combat operations continuing.

title_original: Recap
content_original: Recap Here's a quick recap of some of the most recent events from the Middle East: Gulf tensions At least 131 Iranian cities affected by US-Israeli attacks, the Red Crescent said. Amazon's cloud unit reported a disruption to its services after its UAE-based data centre was struck by "objects", sparking a fire. Kuwaiti air defences mistakenly shot down three US F15 fighter jets flying in Iran-related operations, the US said. Iranian attacks targeted a power plant, a separate energy facility in Qatar, as attacks on civilian infrastructure, including the international airport, were intercepted. One person was killed in an attack on an oil tanker off Muscat that also caused an explosion and a fire. Several US fighter jets crashed in Kuwait on Monday, with all pilots ejecting safely.  France is "ready" to defend Gulf countries and Jordan against Iran if necessary, the French foreign minister said. Lebanon strikes Heavy Israeli strikes killed at least 31 pe...
title_en: Recap: Gulf Tensions Escalate With 131 Iranian Cities Hit, US Jets Downed by Kuwait, Regional Attacks Spread
summary_en: A major escalation in the Iran-US conflict has seen at least 131 Iranian cities affected by US-Israeli attacks, while Iranian retaliation targeted infrastructure across the Gulf. In a significant friendly fire incident, Kuwaiti air defenses mistakenly shot down three US F15 fighter jets conducting Iran-related operations. Additional Iranian attacks struck a power plant and energy facility in Qatar, while an oil tanker attack off Muscat killed one person and raised concerns about maritime security in the Gulf.

classification.location: Gulf region, Middle East, Iran, Kuwait
classification.brief: Major escalation with 131 Iranian cities struck by US-Israeli attacks, Iranian retaliation across Gulf infrastructure, and Kuwaiti friendly fire downing three US F15 jets.

summary_en: Russia, Iran, and China have called on the IAEA Board of Governors to condemn the strikes on Iran, likely referring to recent US military operations against Iranian nuclear facilities and IRGC assets. This diplomatic initiative at the IAEA represents a coordinated effort by the axis of Russia, China, and Iran to internationalize the conflict and pressure the West through multilateral institutions, signaling the broadening geopolitical dimensions of the US-Iran hostilities.

classification.brief: Russia, Iran, and China urge IAEA Board to condemn US military strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities and IRGC assets.

title_en: 4 American soldiers killed in Iran-US conflict; another soldier confirmed dead
summary_en: At least four American soldiers have been confirmed killed in the ongoing Iran-US armed conflict, with another soldier's death just confirmed. The casualties appear to be from Iranian ballistic missile and drone strikes on US bases in Iraq and Syria, as well as attacks on US naval assets in the Gulf region. This marks a significant escalation in US military losses since hostilities began, with the death toll expected to rise as more attacks occur and casualties are verified.

classification.brief: Iranian ballistic missile and drone strikes on US bases in Iraq and Syria, plus attacks on US naval assets in the Gulf, killed at least four American soldiers.

title_original: 📝 Самый чувствительный удар 📝 Иранцы дотянулись до американской ПРО Похоже, информация о повреждении иранцами компоне...
content_original: 📝 Самый чувствительный удар 📝 Иранцы дотянулись до американской ПРО Похоже, информация о повреждении иранцами компонента американской ПРО подтверждается: если в Бахрейне уничтожили лишь станцию спутниковой связи, то на катарской базе Аль-Удейд действительно был поражен радар AN/FPS-132 ценой в $1 млрд. Этот объект — один из ключевых узлов системы ПРО США, который «закрывает» огромный сектор от Европы до Индийского океана . В мире всего шесть AN/FPS-132: кроме Штатов, они также есть в Британии и Гренландии. О технической возможности станции в Аль-Удейде говорит хотя бы то, что она могла обнаруживать пуски ракет на дальности в свыше пяти тысяч километров, в том числе — с территории России. Не говоря уже про взлетающие из Ирана ракеты. 🖍 Насколько критично это для США? Повреждения на снимках не фатальны, и теоретически AN/FPS-132 может быть и восстановлен. К тому же в регионе Штаты обладают огромными разведывательными возможностями, и средств отслеживания иранских пусков у них с лихвой. 🚩...
title_en: Iranians Reach American Missile Defense System
summary_en: Iranian forces have reportedly struck an AN/FPS-132 radar system worth $1 billion at the Al-Udeid base in Qatar, according to multiple sources. This radar is one of only six such systems globally and serves as a critical node in U.S. missile defense, covering a vast sector from Europe to the Indian Ocean with detection capabilities exceeding 5,000 kilometers. The attack represents a significant technical achievement for Iran, as the system could previously detect missile launches including those from Russian territory.

classification.brief: Iranian forces struck a critical $1 billion AN/FPS-132 missile defense radar system at Al-Udeid base in Qatar, destroying one of only six such systems globally that provide missile defense coverage from Europe to the Indian Ocean.

title_en: Fresh footage shows possible moment Khamenei met his end as huge explosion tears through Iranian regime headquarters
summary_en: Video footage reportedly captures the moment a massive explosion struck Iranian regime headquarters, with unconfirmed reports suggesting Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei may have been killed in the blast. The incident represents a potentially catastrophic development in the Iran-US conflict, occurring at the heart of Tehran's power structure. If verified, Khamenei's death would constitute the most significant escalation yet in the ongoing hostilities, potentially triggering major leadership succession chaos within the Iranian regime and IRGC.

classification.brief: Massive explosion at Iranian regime headquarters with unconfirmed reports Supreme Leader Khamenei killed, representing potential direct US decapitation strike against Iranian leadership.

This targeted operation against Hezbollah leadership highlights Israel's strategy of degrading the group's command structure as part of its multi-front war.

title_original: Oil surges as Iran conflict disrupts Middle Eastern supply flow
content_original: Oil prices surged by as much as 13 per cent on Monday after shipping in the crucial Strait of Hormuz was disrupted by retaliatory Iranian attacks following initial bombing by Israel and the United States that killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Brent crude futures rose to as much as $82.37 a barrel, the highest since January 2025, before retreating to be up $5.41, or 7.4pc, to $78.28 by 06:05. US West Texas Intermediate crude climbed to an intraday high of $75.33, up over 12pc and the highest since June, though it later pared gains and was up $4.74, or 7.1pc, at $71.76. Both benchmarks jumped as a sustained exchange of counterattacks damaged tankers and sharply disrupted shipments in the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway between Iran and Oman that connects the Gulf to the Arabian Sea. On a typical day, ships carrying oil equal to about one-fifth of global demand from Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Iraq, Iran, and Kuwait sail through the Strait along with tankers hauling diesel, je...
title_en: Oil surges as Iran conflict disrupts Middle Eastern supply flow
summary_en: Oil prices surged by up to 13% on Monday as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz was disrupted by retaliatory Iranian attacks following Israeli and US bombing that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Brent crude rose to $82.37/barrel (highest since January 2025) before settling at $78.28, while WTI climbed to $75.33 (highest since June) before ending at $71.76. The sustained exchange of counterattacks damaged tankers and sharply disrupted shipping through the critical chokepoint, signaling significant escalation and supply risk in the conflict.

classification.brief: Oil prices surged 13% as Iranian retaliatory attacks disrupted Strait of Hormuz shipping following US-Israeli bombing that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei.

title_en: Bet Shemesh disaster: Baruria and her son Yosef killed by Iranian missile; shelter found to be compliant
summary_en: Two Israeli civilians, Baruria Gloria Cohen and her son Yosef (Yossi) Cohen, were killed when an Iranian missile struck Bet Shemesh. An IDF investigation found that several of the victims were not inside the shelter at the time of impact, while protected rooms (madras) located just 10 meters away where people took cover remained unharmed.

content_original: Nicosia, March 02 (QNA) - ​Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides announced that a ⁠Shahed-type unmanned aerial vehicle struck the British RAF Akrotiri airbase, resulting in minor damage.

summary_en: A Shahed-type Iranian unmanned aerial vehicle struck the British RAF Akrotiri airbase in Cyprus, causing minor damage, according to Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides. The attack targets a key British military installation in the Eastern Mediterranean, representing an expansion of Iranian-linked drone operations against Western allies in the region.

classification.brief: Iranian Shahed-type drone struck British RAF Akrotiri airbase in Cyprus, causing minor damage in expansion of Iranian-linked attacks beyond the Gulf region.

title_original: Trump says he ordered Iran strikes to thwart Tehran's missile program
content_original: WASHINGTON, March 2 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday he ordered the U.S. military to attack Iran to thwart Tehran's nuclear program and a ballistic missile program that he said was growing rapidly. (Reporting by Steve Holland; eduiting by David Ljunggren)
title_en: Trump says he ordered Iran strikes to thwart Tehran's missile program
summary_en: U.S. President Donald Trump announced he ordered military strikes against Iran to destroy Tehran's nuclear program and rapidly developing ballistic missile capabilities. The strikes represent a major escalation in the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict, targeting Iran's strategic weapons infrastructure directly.

classification.brief: Trump ordered major military strikes against Iran to destroy Tehran's nuclear program and ballistic missile capabilities, marking a significant escalation in US-Iran tensions.

title_original: Stranded tourists shelter from missile fire in Dubai
content_original: On Dubai's artificial island, empty beaches stand in stark contrast to frantic hotel lobbies, where stranded tourists have taken shelter as they wait for a way home. Iran's strikes on Gulf neighbours since Saturday, following the US-Israeli attack, forced the UAE to shut its airspace, blindsiding travellers who thought they were headed to one of the region's safest holiday destinations. Dubai, which welcomed nearly 20 million visitors in 2025, has built its brand on safety and the global reach of its busy airport.
title_en: Stranded tourists shelter from missile fire in Dubai
summary_en: Iranian missile strikes on Gulf neighbors since Saturday forced the UAE to shut its airspace, stranding tourists in Dubai who had arrived seeking what was marketed as one of the region's safest holiday destinations. The attacks, following the US-Israeli attack on Iran, have disrupted operations at Dubai International Airport, which handled nearly 20 million visitors in 2025, demonstrating the conflict's spillover effects on civilian infrastructure and regional commerce.

classification.brief: Iranian ballistic missile strikes on Gulf neighbor UAE forced airspace closure, stranding tourists in Dubai, in retaliation for US-Israeli attack on Iran.

title_original: From missiles to minerals: The strategic meaning behind the Iran strike
content_original: The death of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei , following coordinated U.S.–Israeli strikes in late February 2026 marks one of the most consequential geopolitical moments of the decade. In the immediate aftermath, Iran launched retaliatory missile and drone attacks across Israel and against U.S. and Gulf-linked infrastructure, while internet disruptions spread domestically and internal unrest intensified. Analysts, journalists and policymakers quickly filled the information space with competing interpretations — some emphasizing escalation risks, others focusing on humanitarian fallout or regime durability. Yet viewed through the lens increasingly guiding U.S. national security doctrine, the operation appears less as an isolated military escalation and more as part of a broader strategic transition already underway: the integration of economic security, technological dominance and supply-chain resilience into core American grand strategy. Over the past five years, Washingto...
title_en: From missiles to minerals: The strategic meaning behind the Iran strike
summary_en: Coordinated US-Israeli strikes in late February 2026 killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, marking a major escalation in the conflict. Iran responded with retaliatory missile and drone attacks across Israel and against US and Gulf-linked infrastructure, while internal unrest and internet disruptions spread domestically. The event represents one of the most consequential geopolitical moments of the decade, fundamentally altering the regional strategic landscape.

title_original: Three killed, 58 injured in Iranian attack on UAE
content_original: A wave of Iranian missiles and suicide drones struck targets across the United Arab Emirates (UAE) on Sunday, leaving three civilians dead and 58 others injured. The UAE’s Ministry of Defense confirmed that the deceased were expatriate workers from Pakistan, Nepal, and Bangladesh, marking the first confirmed fatalities on Emirati soil since the regional conflict escalated following US and Israeli strikes on Iran. Scale of the assault Emirati Air Defense systems faced an unprecedented test over the last 48 hours. Military data revealed that 165 ballistic missiles were launched from Iran toward the UAE. While the vast majority—152 projectiles—were intercepted and destroyed, 13 fell into the Arabian Gulf. The drone swarm was even more extensive. Of the 541 Iranian drones detected, 506 were neutralized by defense systems. However, 35 drones managed to impact within UAE territory, striking civilian areas and causing varying degrees of structural damage.   Capital impacted In Abu ...

summary_en: Iran launched 165 ballistic missiles and suicide drones at targets across the UAE, with Emirati air defenses intercepting 152 projectiles while 13 struck civilian areas, killing three expatriate workers from Pakistan, Nepal, and Bangladesh and injuring 58 others. The attack marks the first confirmed fatalities on Emirati soil since the regional conflict escalated and represents an unprecedented test of UAE air defense capabilities.

title_original: Iran continues to strike Gulf in retaliation to US-Israel war
content_original: Iran launched retaliatory missile and drone strikes across multiple Gulf states on Monday for a third consecutive day, saying it was targeting American military assets in response to sustained US and Israeli attacks on the country. The US–Israeli campaign has struck military, political and infrastructure targets across Iran since the weekend, including the assassination of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other senior regime leaders, but also seen massive harm to civilians, including the killing of around 160 children following a strike on a school on Saturday. Iranian authorities say at least 555 people have been killed and hundreds more wounded in the three-day war, describing its response as an act of self-defence. Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Iran was "not attacking our neighbours" but was "attacking American targets" in retaliation for what he called American aggression. title_original: One person killed as oil tanker hit off Oman coast
content_original: One person killed as oil tanker hit off Oman coast One person was killed in an attack on an oil tanker off Muscat that also caused an explosion and a fire, the Omani authorities said Monday, as Iran presses on with its Gulf retaliation campaign. “The oil tanker MKD VYOM, flying the flag of the Republic of the Marshall Islands, was attacked by an unmanned boat 52 nautical miles off the coast of Muscat Governorate. The tanker was carrying approximately 59,463 metric tons of cargo,” the Omani maritime security center said in a statement. “The attack resulted in a fire and explosion in the main engine room, killing one crew member, an Indian national,” it added.
title_en: One person killed as oil tanker hit off Oman coast
summary_en: An unmanned boat attacked the oil tanker MKD VYOM (Marshall Islands flag) carrying approximately 59,463 metric tons of cargo, 52 nautical miles off Muscat, Oman. The attack caused a fire and explosion in the main engine room, killing one Indian crew member. The incident is part of Iran's ongoing Gulf retaliation campaign, targeting oil tankers in strategic shipping lanes.

classification.location: 52 nautical miles off Muscat, Oman (Arabian Sea/Gulf of Oman)
classification.brief: Unmanned boat attacked oil tanker MKD VYOM causing fire and explosion, killing one Indian crew member in incident linked to Iran-aligned forces.

title_original: UK PM Starmer says Iran stepping up 'reckless' strikes after Khamenei’s killing
content_original: LONDON, March 2 (Reuters) - British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday that the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has led Iran to strike military and economic targets in a more "reckless" manner in the region. The death of Khamenei "will not stop Iran from launching these strikes," Starmer told parliament. "In fact, their approach is becoming even more reckless and more dangerous to civilians." (Reporting by William James and Sam Tabahriti, writing by Andrew MacAskill, editing by Alistair Smout)
title_en: UK PM Starmer says Iran stepping up 'reckless' strikes after Khamenei's killing
summary_en: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Iran is conducting more reckless strikes against military and economic targets following the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Starmer warned that Khamenei's death would not stop Iran from launching these attacks and that the approach is becoming increasingly dangerous to civilians. This represents a significant escalation signal in the ongoing Iran-US conflict with broader regional implications.

classification.brief: Classified by keyword matching (LLM unavailable)

content_original: شفقنا- خبرگزاری رویترز به نقل از کمیسیون عملیات تجارت دریایی بریتانیا گزارش داد که یک حادثه امنیتی در بندر بحرین رخ داده است. به گزارش شفقنا؛ وبگاه شبکه الجزیره نوشت:‌ «بر اساس این گزارش، دو پرتابه به یک کشتی اصابت کرده و باعث آتش‌سوزی در آن شده است. جزئیات بیشتر درباره خسارت یا تلفات انسانی […] نوشته رویترز: حمله به یک کشتی در بندر بحرین باعث آتش‌سوزی شد اولین بار در خبرگزاری بین‌المللی شفقنا | خبرگزاری شیعیان، فتاوای آیت‌الله سیستانی و مرجعیت، آخرین اخبار ایران و جهان . پدیدار شد.

title_en: RAF airbase in Cyprus hit as Cooper defends decision to allow US to use UK bases for Iran strikes

title_original: Minister says UK 'not at war' after Iranian drone hits UK Cyprus base
content_original: Minister says UK 'not at war' after Iranian drone hits UK Cyprus base An Iranian drone hit the runway of a UK air force base in Cyprus on Monday hours after prime minister Keir Starmer said Britain would not join the US-Israeli conflict with Iran. He said that mistakes of the Iraq war had been "learned". Starmer announced late on Sunday that he had agreed to the United States' request to use British bases for "specific and limited defensive purpose". Middle East Minister Hamish Falconer insisted on Monday that "the UK is not at war". "Let me be really clear: the UK took a deliberate decision not to be part of the first wave of strikes conducted by the United States and Israeli governments. "But in the face of reckless attacks from Iran on a whole range of allies in the region... we took the decision, as the Prime Minister announced last night, to support the US's request to use our bases in order to conduct defensive actions," he added.
title_en: Minister says UK 'not at war' after Iranian drone hits UK Cyprus base
summary_en: An Iranian drone struck the runway of a UK air force base in Cyprus on Monday, marking an expansion of hostilities to include British military assets. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer had previously stated Britain would not join the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, and Middle East Minister Hamish Falconer insisted the UK is 'not at war' despite the attack, emphasizing that British bases were only authorized for 'specific and limited defensive purposes' at US request.

classification.brief: Iranian drone struck a UK air force base in Cyprus, expanding hostilities beyond US-Israeli targets and testing UK's stated refusal to join the conflict.

And yet it is a question that would be far less hypocritically posed by, say, a newspaper that had not once run an opinion piece by John Bolton, the former US ambassador to the United Natio...

content_original: شفقنا – مقامات قبرس و وزارت دفاع بریتانیا اعلام کردند که پایگاه نیروی هوایی سلطنتی بریتانیا، آکروتیری، در قبرس مورد حمله پهپادی قرار گرفته و خسارات محدودی به بار آمد ولی تلفات جانی در پی نداشته است. به گزارش سرویس ترجمه شفقنا، در یک هشدار امنیتی که توسط مدیریت پایگاه بریتانیایی برای ساکنان اطراف آکروتیری […] نوشته پایگاه نیروی هوایی بریتانیا در قبرس هدف حمله پهپادی قرار گرفت/لندن اجازه استفاده از پایگاه‌های نظامی خود برای حمله به تهران را صادر کرد اولین بار در خبرگزاری بین‌المللی شفقنا | خبرگزاری شیعیان، فتاوای آیت‌الله سیستانی و مرجعیت، آخرین اخبار ایران و جهان . پدیدار شد.

title_original: Death toll from girls’ school attack in Iran surges to 148
content_original: • Israeli military says ‘unaware of the attack’ • US claims strike reports being looked into • Malala urges all states, parties to ‘safeguard schools’ PARIS: The death toll from the Israeli strikes that hit a girls’ school in southern Iran surged to 148, with nearly 100 others wounded, Al Jazeera reported. While Iranian officials claimed the attack on the Shajareh Tayyebeh girls’ elementary school in the Minab city killed more than 100 people, neither the United States nor Israel confirmed that any such attack has taken place. Israel’s military on Sunday said it was “not aware” of any US or Israeli strike on any school in Iran. When asked about the strikes on school, Israel’s military spokesman Lt-Col Nadav Shoshani said, “At this point not aware of an Israeli or an American strike there… We’re operating in an extremely accurate manner.” However, The New York Times later quoted a CENTCOM spokesperson as saying: “We are aware of reports concerning civilian harm resulting from ongoing mi...
title_en: Death toll from girls' school attack in Iran surges to 148
summary_en: Iranian officials claim Israeli strikes hit the Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school in Minab city, southern Iran, killing 148 people and wounding nearly 100. Neither the United States nor Israel has confirmed any such strike occurred; Israel's military stated it is 'not aware' of any Israeli or US strike on a school in Iran. The incident, if verified, would represent a significant escalation and potential war crime involving civilian (children's) targeting, though Israeli denial suggests possible Iranian disinformation or misattribution.

classification.brief: Iranian officials claim Israeli airstrike on girls' school killed 148 people; Israel denies involvement, no US confirmation

title_original: Pakistan urges restraint as missiles rain down on Gulf
content_original: • PM speaks to Jordan, Bahrain rulers, offers support to restore calm; Dar contacts Omani, Iraqi counterparts • Loud blasts heard in Dubai and Doha; Oman hit for first time; multiple Gulf ports targeted • Emirati, Kuwaiti stock markets closed amid crisis • UAE confirms Pakistani national killed by missile debris ISLAMABAD: Amid Ira­nian counterattacks against US assets in the Gulf, Pakistan reached out to the rulers of Jordan and Bahrain via telephone and urged the need for restraint, de-escalation, and dialogue to preserve regional peace and stability. The Gulf states, which house American assets, came under attack after Iran retaliated to joint US-Israeli strikes, which killed its top leadership, including the supreme leader, and scores of citizens. According to the PM Office, the PM held a telephone conversation with Jordan’s King Abdullah II to discuss the evolving regional situation. The two leaders exchanged views on the serious regional developments following the recent escalati...

title_original: Khamenei’s assassination brings world to precipice of chaos
content_original: • Iran confirms supreme leader’s death, declares 40-day mourning • Hospital targeted as Israeli missiles pound Tehran, other areas for second day; retaliatory strikes kill 9 in Israel • US claims sinking 9 Iranian warships, B-2 bombers target missile sites; 3 American troops killed • Shipping paralysed as IRGC closes Strait of Hormuz • Trump says war on Iran ‘could last four weeks’; Tehran still open to talks, insists on ‘no limit’ to self-defence JERUSALEM: The war between Iran and the US-Israel alliance escalated sharply on Sunday after Iran confirmed the assassination of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khame­nei in a joint US-Israeli airstrike, a move that has plunged the Middle East into its most perilous crisis in decades. As Iranian government declared 40 days of national mourning and seven public holidays following the death of the man who wielded absolute power since 1989, Iranian missiles killed at least nine people in a central Israeli town, while the US and Israel continued bom...
title_en: Khamenei's assassination brings world to precipice of chaos
summary_en: A joint US-Israeli airstrike assassinated Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, confirmed by Tehran which declared 40-day mourning. Israeli missiles continued pounding Tehran and other areas for a second day, while Iran retaliatory strikes killed 9 in Israel. The US claims to have sunk 9 Iranian warships and used B-2 bombers to target missile sites, though 3 American troops were also killed in the escalation. IRGC has closed the Strait of Hormuz, paralyzing shipping, as Trump warned the war 'could last four weeks' while Tehran insisted it remains open to talks but will exercise 'no limit' to self-defence.

classification.brief: Joint US-Israeli airstrike assassinated Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei, with Israeli missiles continuing to strike Tehran for a second day and Iran retaliating with strikes that killed 9 in Israel.

Наваф Салам , премьер-министр страны, уже заявил, что так «Хезболла» сделала только хуже, а правительство сделает все, чтобы отвести конфликт от Ливана. 🖍 Вместе с этим, очевидно, в Израиле на сегодняшних ударах не остановятся. title_original: Iran's IRGC has issued its ninth statement regarding Operation True Promise 4:  According to the statement, "The tent...
content_original: Iran's IRGC has issued its ninth statement regarding Operation True Promise 4: According to the statement, "The tenth wave opened massive gates of fire upon the occupied territories with a maneuver of Khaibar missiles." The IRGC stated that the wave specifically targeted the governmental complex of the Zionist regime in Tel Aviv, military and security centers in Haifa, and sites in East al-Quds. In a warning to Israeli settlers, the IRGC advised: "We recommend that residents of the occupied territories distance themselves from military bases, security centers, and government facilities, and immediately leave the occupied territories." The statement added that earlier warnings had been given that "the sounds of sirens in Israel will never stop." @PressTV

summary_en: Iran's IRGC announced the tenth wave of Operation True Promise 4, using Khaibar missiles to target the governmental complex in Tel Aviv, military and security centers in Haifa, and sites in East Jerusalem. The IRGC warned Israeli settlers to distance themselves from military, security, and government facilities, continuing Iran's ballistic missile campaign against Israel as part of the ongoing Iran-US/Israel conflict.

classification.brief: IRGC announced tenth wave of ballistic missile strikes targeting Israeli governmental and military sites using Khaibar missiles.

content_original: Three killed in US-Israeli strikes on western Iran, state media says Strikes on western Iran left at least three people dead on Monday, Iranian state media reported, on the third day of the United States and Israel attacks on the country. Residential buildings in the city of Sanandaj were hit by airstrikes, according to Governor Gharib Sajjadi, quoted by the official news agency Irna. "Three people have been killed and several others injured," he said, adding that the death toll was expected to rise.

title_original: חיזבאללה מצטרף למלחמה
content_original: חיזבאללה בחר בימים האחרונים במהלך מחושב וזהיר: שיגורים בודדים מלבנון לעבר הצפון והמרכז, רובם לשטחים פתוחים וללא נפגעים, שנועדו לשדר מעורבות – אך לא הידרדרות. מאחורי הירי המוגבל מסתתר ניסיון לאזן בין מחויבות פומבית לאיראן לבין רצון מובהק להימנע ממלחמה רחבה נוספת מול ישראל. בארגון מבקשים להוכיח כי עמדו בהתחייבותם להגיב על חיסולו של המנהיג העליון האיראני, אך בפועל הקפידו על מינון שמפחית את הסיכון להסלמה כוללת. המסר המשתמע: היכולת קיימת, אך הכוונה היא לסמן נוכחות בלבד ולא לפתוח חזית כוללת. הרקע לצעדים הזהירים נעוץ גם בזירה הפנימית בלבנון. ביקורת חריפה נשמעה מצד הדרג המדיני בביירות, ובראשה ראש הממשלה נוואף סלאם, שהזהיר מפני גרירת המדינה לעימותים חדשים. ההתנגדות אינה מוגבלת לממשלה או לעדות אחרות בלבד; גם בתוך הקהילה השיעית נשמעות הסתייגויות מהסתבכות צבאית נוספת שעלולה להמיט הרס נוסף על המדינה השברירית ממילא. מנגד, ישראל כבר הגיבה בתקיפות בדאחייה שבביירות והעבירה מסרים תקיפים לתושבי עשרות כפרים בדרום לבנון. במקביל גויסו כ־100 אלף אנשי מילואים, ורבים מהם מוצבים בגבולות לבנון וסוריה. האפשרות של...

title_original: Monday briefing: ​What does the escalation in the Middle East mean for global stability?
content_original: In today’s newsletter: A region already strained by years of proxy conflict is now confronting its most volatile moment in decades, as the death of Iran’s supreme leader ​could drag us into a rapidly widening crisis, with no clear path to de‑escalation Good morning. Over the weekend the US and Israel launched a series of attacks on Iran that resulted in the assassination of Tehran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei – a move that dramatically raised the stakes in their long-running conflict. The assassination of a serving supreme leader is an extraordinary act – one that signals not containment, but confrontation. Retaliatory strikes by Iran have already spilled the conflict far beyond its borders, drawing a host of regional powers into the fray. Iranian president, Masoud Pezeshkian, the judiciary chief, Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, and senior cleric Alireza Arafi will reportedly lead Iran in the transitional period following Khamenei’s death. Iran | Donald Trump said on Sunday he ...
title_en: Monday briefing: What does the escalation in the Middle East mean for global stability?
summary_en: The US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran resulting in the assassination of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, marking the most volatile moment in decades for a region already strained by proxy conflicts. Iranian retaliatory strikes have already spread the conflict far beyond its initial boundaries, with no clear path to de-escalation.

classification.location: Iran and wider Middle East region
classification.brief: US and Israel conducted coordinated strikes on Iran assassinating Supreme Leader Khamenei, triggering Iranian retaliatory strikes in what is described as the most volatile Middle East moment in decades.

title_original: 📝 Флоту здесь не место 📝 снова удары по Бахрейну Из всех пораженных иранцами целей на территории монархий Персидского...
content_original: 📝 Флоту здесь не место 📝 снова удары по Бахрейну Из всех пораженных иранцами целей на территории монархий Персидского залива минувшей ночью сильнее прочих досталось снова Бахрейну . В Сети опубликовали кадры чадящей базы 5-го флота ВМС США в Джуффейре . Всего было 9-10 прилётов. На картах FIRMS очаги активного возгорания тоже есть. Очевидцы отмечают почти полное отсутствие работы систем ПВО над американскими объектами. Скорее всего, сказались проблемы в логистике и рассогласованности действий, потому как предположить, что в пускай и очень небольшой монархии полностью закончились ЗУРы, довольно сложно. К тому же, над промзоной Салман срабатывания ПВО были, только вот обломки убили бедолагу-мигранта из Азии. #Бахрейн #Иран 🧨 @rybar_mena — о ближневосточном хаосе с любовью 💸 Поддержать нас
title_en: "The Fleet Has No Place Here" - Again Strikes on Bahrain
summary_en: Iranian forces struck the US 5th Fleet naval base in Juffair, Bahrain with 9-10 impacts during the night. FIRMS satellite data confirms active fire hotspots at the base. Witnesses report almost complete failure of air defense systems over American facilities, likely due to logistics and coordination problems rather than exhaustion of air defense missiles in the small monarchy. One civilian was killed by debris in the Salman industrial zone where air defense did activate.

content_original: לאחר תקופה ארוכה של שקט יחסי, ארגון הטרור חיזבאללה שבר הלילה (שני) באופן רשמי את הסכם הפסקת האש ופתח במערכה רחבת היקף מול ישראל. הירי כלל מטחים כבדים של רקטות ושיגור כטב"מים מתאבדים, כאשר בארגון הטרור טענו כי היעד המרכזי היה בסיס צבאי דרומית לחיפה. בעקבות ההסלמה, קיים הרמטכ"ל, רב-אלוף אייל זמיר, הערכת מצב דחופה עם פורום המטה הכללי. במהלך הישיבה אישר הרמטכ"ל את התוכניות המבצעיות להמשך הלחימה והכריז על פתיחת מבצע "שאגת הארי". "חיזבאללה פתח הלילה במערכה מול ישראל, והאחריות על ההסלמה הזו מוטלת עליו ורק עליו", הצהיר הרמטכ"ל זמיר. "נערכנו לתרחיש רב-זירתי ולמערכה התקפית עוצמתית. כל אויב שיאיים על ביטחוננו ישלם מחיר כבד מאוד. לא נאפשר פגיעה בתושבי הצפון". בחיזבאללה מיהרו לקחת אחריות על הירי וטענו כי מדובר בתגובה ישירה לחיסולו הדרמטי של המנהיג העליון של איראן, עלי חמינאי, וכן על התקיפות הישראליות האחרונות בשטחי לבנון וסוריה. זוהי הפעם הראשונה שהארגון מבצע ירי תלול-מסלול לעבר ישראל מאז כניסת ההסכמים לתוקף בסוף שנת 2024. בצה"ל הנחו על היערכות...

title_original: No intel suggested that Iran planned to attack first, Pentagon tells Congress
content_original: No intel suggested that Iran planned to attack first, Pentagon tells Congress Pentagon officials told the US Congress that there were no signs that Iran planned to attack the US before the US-Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, news agency Reuters has reported. Officials said that Iran’s ballistic missiles posed an imminent threat to US interests, but they acknowledged that there was no intelligence suggesting that Tehran planned to attack US forces first.
title_en: No intel suggested that Iran planned to attack first, Pentagon tells Congress
summary_en: Pentagon officials told Congress there was no intelligence indicating Iran planned to attack US forces first before the US-Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. While officials claimed Iran's ballistic missiles posed an imminent threat to US interests, they acknowledged the lack of specific intelligence showing Tehran intended to strike first. This testimony relates directly to the justification for the US-Israeli military actions against Iran.

classification.brief: Pentagon told Congress no intelligence indicated Iran planned to attack US forces first before US-Israeli strikes killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

content_original: 📝 Венесуэла 2.0 📝 4-5 недель и такой же результат? Ночью американские и израильские войска продолжили наносить массированные удары по Ирану . Самые жестокие удары пришлись по Тегерану (особенно западной части), а также столичному пригороду Караджу . Одной из целей стало министерство разведки. Обрубили связь в государственной телерадиокомпании Seda wa Sima . Попали по штаб-квартире МВД в Аббасабаде. Прилетело и по Санандаджу в иранском Курдистане, и южным Бандар-Аббасу с Ширазом , и Керманшаху . В Чобахаре ударили по объектам КСИР. Взрывы зафиксированы на неких объектах в Бушере , Ахвазе , Тебризе , Абадане . ➡️ Иранцы заявили, что сумели сбить ещё один американский БЛА MQ-9 Reaper над Исфаханом . Трампа же якобы проинформировали о 9 затопленных иранских военных кораблях. ➡️ Помимо обмена ударами в Сети происходил обмен отчетными роликами. В Пентагоне опубликовали видео поражения иранской авиации. В Иране в долгу не остались и опубликовали нарезку запусков дронов и ракет. ➡️ Трамп в ноч...

title_original: Morning recap
content_original: Morning recap Good morning, Middle East Eye readers. Events are still unfolding rapidly across the Middle East, so here's a quick recap: President Donald Trump has suggested that the Israeli-US war on Iran could continue for up to a month. The United Kingdom has agreed to a US request to use British military bases for what Prime Minister Keir Starmer claimed are “defensive” strikes on Iranian missile sites. Mass displacement has been reported in south Lebanon after Israeli attacks as Hezbollah joins the war. More than 20 people were killed in a US-Israeli attack on Tehran’s Niloofar Square, as explosions were heard across the capital.   The death toll from the US-Israeli strike on a girls’ school in Minab has climbed to 180.  Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has dismissed Trump’s warning against retaliation, vowing that Tehran will place “no limit” on its right to defend itself.  The US military says it has hit more than 1,000 targets across Iran since launchi...
title_en: Morning Recap: Trump Suggests Month-Long War; UK Backs US Strikes; 20+ Killed in Tehran Attack
summary_en: President Trump suggested the Israeli-US war on Iran could last up to a month, while the UK agreed to allow US use of British military bases for defensive strikes on Iranian missile sites. Over 20 people were killed in a US-Israeli attack on Tehran's Niloofar Square, with explosions heard across the capital, as mass displacement continues in south Lebanon with Hezbollah joining the conflict.

classification.brief: US-Israeli attack on Tehran's Niloofar Square killed over 20 people while UK agreed to allow US use of British bases for strikes on Iranian missile sites, with Trump suggesting the war could last up to a month.

title_original: Oil price surges, stocks drop on Iran conflict
content_original: Oil prices soared and stocks fell in Asia on Monday on the back of turmoil in the Middle East after US and Israeli strikes on Iran , with the vital Strait of Hormuz effectively shut and several ships attacked. In early trade in Asia, Brent Crude rocketed to just over $80 per barrel compared to the closing price of $72.87 on Friday, before easing slightly to below $79. On stock markets, Japan's Nikkei was down 2.2 percent in early trade and Sydney was 0.5 percent lower. Gold, a safe haven investment, rose two percent. Brent, the international benchmark for crude, already rose last week ahead of the strikes that began on Saturday and which killed supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. US President Donald Trump has called on Iranians to rise up against their government and said the war could last "four weeks". Iran's retaliatory missile and drone campaign in the Gulf killed four people and wounded dozens more, the UAE foreign ministry said. While Iran has not officially closed the Strait ...
title_en: Oil price surges, stocks drop on Iran conflict
summary_en: US and Israeli strikes on Iran caused oil prices to surge to just over $80 per barrel (up from $72.87) and Asian stock markets to fall, with Japan's Nikkei down 2.2%. The vital Strait of Hormuz has been effectively shut and several ships attacked, while gold rose 2% as investors sought safe havens. The strikes reportedly killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

classification.brief: US and Israeli strikes on Iran shut Strait of Hormuz and attacked several ships, causing oil prices to surge to $80 and Asian stocks to fall.

content_original: Air attack reported south of Baghdad, Al Jazeera says An air attack on the Jurf al-Sakhr area south of the capital, Baghdad, took place early Monday morning, Al Jazeera reported, citing security sources.  Widespread displacement is also ongoing in south Lebanon, following exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah, with residents leaving the Tyre area for Beirut and northern regions. The reports came amid ongoing strikes on the Iranian capital, after the United States and Israel carried out a joint attack on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. content_original: Bahrain says missile debris from 'Iranian aggression' caused deadly ship fire Bahrain’s Interior Ministry said falling debris from an intercepted missile caused a fire on a foreign vessel in the industrial zone near Mina Salman port in Manama. One Asian worker was killed and two others were seriously injured, the ministry said, in what it described as “Iranian aggression".  The fire was later brought under control by civil defence teams.  عاجل بالفيديو | العدوان الإيراني يستهدف إحدى المنشآت البحرية بالقرب من ميناء سلمان والدفاع المدني يباشر إجراءاته للسيطرة على الحريق pic.twitter.com/0WKFV7suFG — صحيفة البلاد البحرينية (@albiladpress) March 1, 2026
title_en: Bahrain says missile debris from 'Iranian aggression' caused deadly ship fire
summary_en: Debris from an intercepted Iranian missile struck a foreign vessel in the industrial zone near Mina Salman port in Manama, Bahrain, causing a fire that killed one Asian worker and injured two others. The incident marks the first direct hit on Bahraini territory during the current Iran-US conflict, occurring at a port facility in the capital. Bahrain, which hosts the US Navy's 5th Fleet and a major US air base, described the attack as 'Iranian aggression,' demonstrating the widening geographic scope of Iranian ballistic missile and drone strikes beyond US military installations.

classification.brief: Iranian missile debris struck a foreign vessel in Bahrain's port, killing one worker and injuring two others in the first direct hit on Bahraini territory during the Iran-US conflict.

title_original: Video: Iranian missile strike damages Jerusalem, wounds seven
content_original: Video: Iranian missile strike damages Jerusalem, wounds seven Widespread destruction was left across Jerusalem after an Iranian missile strike, with debris strewn across streets as emergency crews rushed to the scene. Video from the aftermath showed a large crater and a wave of ambulances and rescue vehicles converging on the impact site. Seven people were wounded in the attack, one of them critically, according to emergency services. The strike came amid an intensifying round of regional hostilities following major US and Israeli attacks against Iran. In those strikes, Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed along with several senior commanders, prompting Iran to launch retaliatory missiles toward Israel and other US targets in the region. title_original: US mass shooting possibly motivated by Iran strikes – media (VIDEOS)
content_original: The FBI is investigating the Austin, Texas incident as a potential act of terrorism The gunman who killed two people and injured over a dozen in Texas may have been motivated by US strikes on Iran that killed the country’s Supreme Leader and caused widespread civilian casualties, according to law enforcement sources cited by US media. The attack occurred in a busy nightlife district in Austin near the University of Texas shortly before 2am on Sunday. The suspect, identified as 53-year-old Ndiaga Diagne, opened fire outside Buford’s Backyard Beer Garden, killing two people and injuring at least 14 others. The naturalized US citizen, originally from Senegal, drove past the venue several times before stopping and firing a pistol from his SUV at people gathered outside, according to Austin Police Chief Lisa Davis. 🚨 BREAKING: Shocking footage captures the moment the shooter opened fire inside an Austin bar pic.twitter.com/ANYjIIQ5fs — Breaking911 (@Breaking911) March 1, 2...
title_en: US mass shooting possibly motivated by Iran strikes – media

Law enforcement sources cited by US media suggest the attacker may have been motivated by US strikes on Iran that killed the Iranian Supreme Leader and caused civilian casualties, potentially representing spillover terrorism connected to the ongoing Iran-US conflict.

title_original: 5 takeaways from the first 2 days of US-Israel war against Iran

The conflict – triggered by US-Israeli strikes that killed Iran’s supreme leader and senior officials – is rapidly reshaping regional security calculations and global economic outlooks. title_original: UAE halts stock markets for two days after Iran strikes
content_original: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has ordered its stock markets closed on Monday and Tuesday as the country reels from Iran’s retaliatory missile and drone strikes , in a sign of the growing economic disruption sweeping the Gulf. Iran carried out the strikes in Gulf countries that have US bases and assets after joint attacks on the Islamic republic by Israel and America. The UAE Capital Markets Authority said the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange and Dubai Financial Market would remain shut on March 2 and March 3, citing its supervisory and regulatory role over the country’s capital markets. “The Authority will continue to monitor developments in the region and assess the situation on an ongoing basis, taking any further measures as necessary,” it said in a statement. The UAE’s two exchanges are home to some of the region’s most valuable listed companies. title_original: Starmer says US can use UK bases for missile strikes on Iran
content_original: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday he had agreed to let the United States use UK bases to fire "defensive" strikes aimed at destroying Iranian missiles and their launchers. But in a video address posted to social media, he added: "We were not involved in the initial strikes on Iran and we will not join offensive action now. "But Iran is pursuing a scorched-earth strategy, so we are supporting the collective self-defence of our allies and our people in the region." Starmer added: "Our decision that the UK would not be involved with the strikes on Iran was deliberate. "Not least because we believe that the best way forward for the region and for the world is a negotiated settlement." A separate statement posted on the UK government website set out its legal position. Starmer said "the only way to stop the threat" from Iran was "to destroy the missiles at source - in their storage depots or the launchers which are used to fire the missiles". title_original: Khamenei killing: America and Israel cross a new line in international politics
content_original: The killing of a head of state and the end of old restraints Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is dead and the international system is entering a far more dangerous phase than many appear willing to acknowledge. One may hold any opinion about the Islamic Republic of Iran, about its ideology or ruling elite. There are ample grounds for criticism, some severe. Yet one basic fact remains: Ali Khamenei was the legitimate head of a UN member state, recognized by virtually the entire international community, and a lawful participant in international relations. This included ongoing political negotiations with those who ultimately organized the attack, negotiations that continued until the moment the hostilities began. The targeted destruction of a state’s leadership by another state as a matter of deliberate policy marks a fundamentally new stage in world politics. This is not merely another episode of regime change. Even when compared with the brutal ends of Muammar Gaddafi or ...

title_original: US officials skeptical of regime change in Tehran after Khamenei killing, say sources
content_original: By Gram Slattery and Erin Banco March 1 (Reuters) - Following the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday, many senior U.S. officials remain skeptical that the U.S. and Israeli military operation against the Islamic Republic will lead to a regime change in the near term. Before and after the start of the attack, U.S. officials, including U.S. President Donald Trump, had suggested that toppling the nation's repressive governing system was one of several U.S. goals, in addition to crippling Iran's ballistic missile and nuclear programs.
title_en: US officials skeptical of regime change in Tehran after Khamenei killing
summary_en: Following the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday, senior U.S. officials remain skeptical that the U.S. and Israeli military operation against the Islamic Republic will lead to regime change in the near term. Before and after the attack began, U.S. officials including President Trump had suggested that toppling Iran's governing system was a goal alongside crippling its ballistic missile and nuclear programs.

classification.brief: US and Israeli military operation killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with US officials skeptical that this strike will lead to regime change in Tehran.

summary_en: Iran launched retaliatory strikes targeting US military bases and installations across multiple regional countries, including the UAE. The attacks forced the closure of tourist sites like Jebel Jais and prompted remote work advisories in the UAE, demonstrating Iran's strategy to expand the conflict's impact beyond purely military targets to civilian infrastructure and economic centers.

classification.brief: Iran launched retaliatory ballistic missile strikes against US military bases in the UAE and other regional countries, forcing tourist site closures and remote work advisories.

title_original: US cleared to use British bases for limited strikes on Iranian missile capabilities
content_original: The U.S. has been cleared to use British bases for limited strikes on Iran’s missile capabilities after Prime Minister Keir Starmer signed off on the plan, and while U.K. Defense Secretary John Healey stated on Sunday Britain had "stepped up alongside the Americans." "The only way to stop the threat is to destroy the missiles at source, in their storage depots or the launchers which are used to fire the missiles," Starmer confirmed in a recorded statement to the nation. "The U.S. has requested permission to use British bases for that specific and limited defensive purpose," he said. "We have taken the decision to accept this request." The decision came amid escalation across the Middle East in the wake of U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran and Tehran’s retaliatory missile and drone attacks, raising fears of a broader regional conflict. HORMUZ ERUPTS: ATTACKS, GPS JAMMING, HOUTHI THREATS ROCK STRAIT AMID US-ISRAELI STRIKES On Feb. 28, in the wake of Operation Epic Fury, Starmer confirmed ...
title_en: US cleared to use British bases for limited strikes on Iranian missile capabilities
summary_en: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has authorized the US to use British bases for limited strikes targeting Iran's missile capabilities, including storage depots and launchers. The decision marks a significant escalation, with Britain directly supporting US military operations against Iranian military infrastructure. Starmer stated this was necessary to "destroy the missiles at source" to counter the threat.

classification.brief: UK authorizes US to use British bases for limited strikes on Iranian missile storage depots and launchers, marking direct US-UK military action against Iranian military infrastructure.

title_original: Israeli military launches strikes against Hezbollah after group attacks Israel
content_original: By Laila Bassam, Yomna Ehab and Nayera Abdallah March 2 (Reuters) - Israeli military said on Monday it was striking targets of the Iran-backed Hezbollah militants across Lebanon, after the group launched missiles and drones towards Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. The projectiles launched by the Lebanese militant group were the first since the start of U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.
title_en: Israeli military launches strikes against Hezbollah after group attacks Israel
summary_en: The Israeli military struck Hezbollah targets across Lebanon on March 2, after the Iran-backed militant group launched missiles and drones toward Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. These projectiles marked the first attacks by Hezbollah since the start of U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, signaling the expansion of the conflict beyond direct Iran-U.S. exchanges to include Iranian proxy forces.

classification.brief: Israeli military launched airstrikes against Hezbollah targets across Lebanon after the Iran-backed militant group launched missiles and drones in retaliation for the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader.

title_original: Iranian missile strike damages 200 buildings in Tel Aviv
content_original: Iranian missile strike damages 200 buildings in Tel Aviv An Iranian missile struck Tel Aviv, causing part of a building to collapse and sending shrapnel into a vehicle in the surrounding area, according to Israel’s Channel 14. Haaretz reported that roughly 200 buildings have sustained damage in the latest wave of attacks. Authorities have evacuated hundreds of residents as the fallout from the Israeli-American war on Iran intensifies.

title_original: Iran’s military options limited as it tries to hit back at US-Israeli attack
content_original: Retaliatory strikes have so far been high in volume but mostly not very effective and are likely to become less so US-Israel war on Iran – latest updates In the grim calculus of war, Iran now has to hope it gets lucky. The first hours of the joint US-Israeli assault were catastrophic for the Iranian regime: the supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, killed alongside, it is believed, the minister of defence, the head of the armed forces and the head of the powerful Revolutionary Guards. Iran knew its security apparatus had been compromised during the 12-day war of June 2025 when Israel killed a string of senior military commanders. During January’s street protests, Khamenei was moved away to a secure location for his own safety, yet on Saturday he felt safe enough to hold a security meeting in his compound in Tehran. Continue reading...

title_original: Trump vows to avenge first US deaths as Iran war intensifies
content_original: President Donald Trump vowed Sunday to avenge the first US deaths in the war he launched to topple Iran's cleric-run state, which fired missiles across the Middle East even as the headquarters of its elite Revolutionary Guards was reduced to rubble. Trump said he was planning for around four weeks of conflict after Saturday's massive US and Israeli strikes that killed Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
title_en: Trump vows to avenge first US deaths as Iran war intensifies

Trump indicated planning for approximately four weeks of continued conflict.

classification.brief: Trump vows revenge for first US casualties as Iran launches missiles and US/Israeli strikes destroy IRGC headquarters and kill Iran's supreme leader in major escalation of direct US-Iran warfare.

title_original: How Israeli sleight and US might led to the assassination of Ali Khamenei
content_original: An operation decades in the making took just 60 seconds to carry out, but some question its wisdom The assassination of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was the culmination of decades of painstaking intelligence gathering by Israeli secret services, with crucial technological resources and manpower provided over the last six months by the CIA and other US intelligence services, that culminated in a single concentrated burst of lethal violence to decapitate the Iranian regime, according to experts, veteran spies and officials in Israel and the US. Khamenei was killed along with seven “members of the top Iranian security leadership who had gathered at several locations in Tehran” and around a dozen members of his family and close entourage in near-simultaneous strikes within 60 seconds, military officials in Israel said. Forty other senior Iranian leaders also died in the attack. Continue reading...
title_en: How Israeli sleight and US might led to the assassination of Ali Khamenei
summary_en: Israeli intelligence, with crucial CIA and US intelligence support over six months, assassinated Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei along with seven members of top Iranian security leadership at multiple locations in Tehran. The operation, described as decades in the making, represents a major decapitation strike aimed at collapsing the Iranian regime.

classification.brief: Israeli intelligence with US CIA support assassinated Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and seven senior IRGC officials in a multi-location covert operation in Tehran.

title_original: US troop deaths ignite anger over ‘war for Israel’
content_original: US troop deaths ignite anger over ‘war for Israel’ Three American service members have been killed and five others seriously wounded while participating in the joint US- Israeli  war on  Iran , the Pentagon confirmed on Sunday - marking the first US fatalities since the conflict began. US Central Command said additional personnel suffered minor shrapnel wounds and concussions. “Several others sustained minor shrapnel injuries and concussions - and are in the process of being returned to duty. Major combat operations continue and our response effort is ongoing,” the statement read. NBC News, citing US officials, reported that the attack that killed the soldiers occurred in Kuwait. The deaths have triggered fierce criticism in the United States, where opponents argue that Washington entered a war long pushed by Israel. Read more:  US troop deaths ignite anger over ‘war for Israel’
title_en: US troop deaths ignite anger over 'war for Israel'
summary_en: Three US service members were killed and five seriously wounded in Kuwait during joint US-Israeli operations against Iran, marking the first US fatalities since the conflict began. title_original: UAE shuts Tehran embassy after Iran missile strikes
content_original: UAE shuts Tehran embassy after Iran missile strikes The United Arab Emirates has closed its embassy in Tehran and withdrawn its ambassador after Iranian missile strikes targeted Emirati territory, the foreign ministry said. In a statement shared on social media by Afra Al Hameli, the ministry’s director of strategic communications, the UAE said: “The UAE announces closure of its embassy in Tehran and withdrawal of its ambassador and all members of its diplomatic mission, and condemns the Iranian missile attacks.” Abu Dhabi said it took the step in response to “the blatant Iranian missile attacks that targeted the country’s territories, which constitute aggressive assaults that struck civilian sites, including residential areas, airports, ports, and service facilities, and exposed defenceless civilians to danger”.

summary_en: The United Arab Emirates has closed its embassy in Tehran and withdrawn its ambassador along with all diplomatic staff in response to Iranian missile strikes that targeted Emirati territory. title_original: Gulf businesses reel as Iran strikes trigger regional shutdowns
content_original: Iran's retaliatory strikes across the Gulf have triggered the most widespread business disruption in the region since the pandemic, forcing airport closures, halting port operations and sending shockwaves through financial markets. The attacks, launched in response to a joint US-Israeli assault on Iran , landed across every major state in the Gulf, a region that has spent decades building its reputation as one of the world's most reliable business hubs. Three people were killed by the attacks in the United Arab Emirates, and loud bangs were heard for a second day in Dubai and Abu Dhabi on Sunday. The strikes marked an unprecedented escalation for Dubai, a city whose modern identity was built on being insulated from the region's conflicts. From humble beginnings as a small fishing village, the emirate of Dubai used modest oil revenues to build ports, airports and trade centres before pivoting in the 1990s to luxury tourism, real estate and financial services. "Regionally, the impact acr...
title_en: Gulf businesses reel as Iran strikes trigger regional shutdowns
summary_en: Iran launched retaliatory ballistic missile and drone strikes across the Gulf region in response to a US-Israeli assault on Iran, causing the most widespread business disruption since the pandemic. The attacks forced airport closures, halted port operations across the Gulf, killed three people in the UAE, and caused loud explosions in Dubai and Abu Dhabi for a second consecutive day, marking an unprecedented escalation for the normally stable business hub.

classification.brief: Iran launched retaliatory ballistic missile and drone strikes across the Gulf in response to a US-Israeli assault, causing the most widespread business disruption since the pandemic with airport closures, port shutdowns, and three killed.

title_original: Energy markets on edge as Iran war threatens oil supplies
content_original: Iran's trump card in the event of any US or Israeli war was that it could close the Strait of Hormuz to shipping overnight, thus seriously impacting oil and gas supplies and having the world pressure an aggressor to cease their offensive. Now that this war has become reality, there are signs that it is seriously impacting energy markets, with Iran  hitting tankers, ports, and oil facilities in the oil and gas-rich Gulf region. Shortly after US-Israeli strikes  killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei and senior Iranian figures, Tehran said it was shutting the Strait of Hormuz, with vessels in the area receiving electronic warnings not to cross the waterway, which around 20-30 percent of the world's oil passes through. On Sunday, at least three tankers in Gulf waters were damaged in suspected Iranian attacks, including off the coast of Musandam - a chokehold for global energy supplies. The cost for the region could be huge, with 20 million barrels of oil making its way along t...

title_original: US and Israeli attack on Iran: At least 153 girls killed in strike on school
content_original: US and Israeli attack on Iran: At least 153 girls killed in strike on school At least 153 people, almost all of them young girls, have been killed in an air strike on a primary school in southern  Iran , according to the city’s governor. The attack on Saturday morning hit Shajareh Tayyebeh school in the city of Minab, in Hormozgan province, as the  United States  and  Israel  began launching strikes on targets across Iran. The victims were between seven and 12 years old, according to Iran's Tasnim and Fars news agencies. A staff member at the Minab school, who asked not to be named, told Middle East Eye she remains in shock at the intensity of the attack. Through tears, she said she used to watch the young girls playing at school every day. After today’s strikes, however, she saw their bodies lying on classroom benches and in different corners of the school. Read more:  US and Israeli attack on Iran: At least 153 girls killed in strike on school &nbsp...

title_original: Hormuz erupts: Attacks, GPS jamming, Houthi threats rock Strait amid US-Israeli strikes
content_original: The Strait of Hormuz region became a flashpoint Sunday after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran under Operation Epic Fury triggered electronic warfare activity and multiple "attacks" on vessels along one of the world’s most critical energy waterways, according to reports. The sudden escalation followed a Feb. 28 warning from U.S. maritime authorities urging commercial vessels to avoid strategic waterways if possible, including the Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea, citing heightened security risks. "It is recommended that vessels keep clear of this area if possible," the advisory warned. "The Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz and adjacent waters are the most dangerous place right now for commercial shipping," Jakob P. Larsen, head of maritime security at BIMCO , told Fox News Digital. "Ships in the Persian Gulf are under threat from Iranian attacks," Larsen said.
