Погибли трое , получили тяжелые ранения пятеро американцев, еще несколько - получили травмы. Информации о местонахождении пострадавших или обстоятельствах не было. Таким образом, ни на одном из пораженных Ираном объектов формально пока нет пострадавших. Видимо, базы, которые должны были защищать регион, оказались пустыми . Ведь и с Аль-Удейда , и с Ас-Салема , и с базы 5-ого флота в Бахрейне американцы проводили эвакуацию персонала или за день до атак, или практически сразу после. Впрочем, конфликт еще не окончен, а удары по базам продолжаются. И судя по весьма масштабным пожарам, а некоторые продолжаются уже второй день, далеко не все объекты осмотрены экстренными службами. #Иран #США 🧨 @rybar_mena — о ближневосточном хаосе с любовью 💸 Поддержать нас

title_original: Jihad is coming? What Khamenei’s death means for the region and the world
content_original: Eliminating Supreme Leader doesn’t end the conflict. It transforms it into a matter of principle and raises the odds of a wider Middle East war Overnight, Tehran confirmed the death of the Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, following US and Israeli strikes on his residence early on February 28. In strategic terms, this marks a watershed moment in the architecture of the Middle East conflict. This was not a tactical raid or a calibrated show of force, but a decapitation strike at the very apex of Iran’s state system. The confrontation between Iran on one side and the United States and Israel on the other has now entered a qualitatively new phase. The elimination of a state’s highest political and religious authority during an ongoing military operation is, from Tehran’s perspective, a textbook casus belli. This is no longer a limited exchange of blows. It is a shift toward a far broader and potentially systemic confrontation. From 'dec...

title_original: Strike kills four Iran-backed fighters, anger mounts in Iraq
content_original: Four Iran-backed fighters were killed on Sunday when their base in Iraq was struck as the region became embroiled in violence after the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran. The fighters belong to the Hashed al-Shaabi, or the Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF), an alliance of factions now integrated into the regular army, which also include powerful Iran-backed groups. Baghdad, a key ally of Iran, has announced a three-day mourning period for Iran Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a strike on Tehran on Saturday . Iraq, which has recently regained a sense of stability but has long been a proxy battleground between the US and Iran, warned that it did not want to be dragged into the war. But it did not remain unscathed. An Iraqi security official told AFP that an air strike in the central Diyala province killed four fighters from the PMF, with another eight injured. A PMF source confirmed "the martyrdom of four fighters in an attack on their base" in Diyala. The H...

title_original: Iran drone attack caused fire at base hosting French forces: UAE
content_original: An Iranian drone attack on an Abu Dhabi naval base hosting French forces caused a fire but no casualties, the UAE's defence ministry said Sunday, as Iran struck Gulf countries for a second day. The Iranian drone and missile salvos were in retaliation for an ongoing wave of US and Israeli strikes on the Islamic republic that killed its supreme leader on Saturday. "Specialised teams responded today to an incident resulting from an attack by two Iranian drones on a warehouse at Al Salam Naval Base in Abu Dhabi. The attack caused a fire in two containers of general materials, but there were no casualties," the defence ministry said. The base, also known as Camp de la Paix, is Emirati, but hosts French forces at the UAE's invitation. The French military declined to comment on the reports of the strike on Sunday.

title_original: 📝 Французская база в километре от Лувра 📝 Объята огнем после удара ракет или беспилотников. Пока это, впрочем, происх...
content_original: 📝 Французская база в километре от Лувра 📝 Объята огнем после удара ракет или беспилотников. Пока это, впрочем, происходит в ОАЭ. Целью удара рядом с Лувром оказался именно этот объект. Координаты: 24.52122895, 54.39578592 Camp de la Paix («Лагерь мира») также известный как «Французское военное поселение в ОАЭ» была открыт еще в 2009 году Николя Саркози . Суммарное число французского военного персонала в ОАЭ — порядка 250 военнослужащих, 70 из которых относятся к флоту. База позволяет принимать все корабли ВМС Франции, кроме «Шарля де-Голля» . Французские военные объекты обходились для ОАЭ десятками миллионов евро, и только что явно подорожали. #Иран #США #ОАЭ 🧨 @rybar_mena — о ближневосточном хаосе с любовью 💸 Поддержать нас

title_original: 📝 Мало потопили 📝 Сразу после поражения Skylight появились сообщения об ударах со стороны Исламской республики по еще...
content_original: 📝 Мало потопили 📝 Сразу после поражения Skylight появились сообщения об ударах со стороны Исламской республики по еще двум судам у берегов Омана в Ормузском проливе. ➡️ По данными Reuters со ссылкой на оманский Центр морской безопасности, речь идет о танкере MKD VYOM (ранее известное как Dong Ting Hu и Dania), который ходит под флагом Маршалловых островов. ➡️ О поражении третьего неизвестного судна свидетельствуют данные Центра операций по управлению морскими торговыми операциями Великобритании (UKMTO). 🖍 В случае со Skylight это было попадание по кораблю из теневого флота Ирана. Здесь же удалось избежать огня по своим: у судов подписано соглашение о свободной ассоциации с США. 🚩 По таким целям ранее били йеменские хуситы в Красноморском регионе , обвиняя их в нарушении блокады израильских властей и торговле с «преступным режимом». ❗️ Однако в данном моменте что для иранцев, что для «Ансараллы» цели вполне легальные: морские объекты западных компаний, удобно оказавшиеся в зоне конфликт...

title_original: A visual guide to US-Israeli strikes on Iran – and Tehran’s response
content_original: Missiles and bombs landed across Iran, hitting political and security targets in Tehran, including supreme leader’s residence US-Israeli attack on Iran – live updates US and Israel strikes on Iran – what we know so far The US-Israeli war against Iran entered its second day on Sunday, as news of the assassination of the country’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, shook the Islamic Republic and the wider region. Donald Trump announced Khamenei’s death while Israel claimed to have killed at least 40 senior Iranian commanders in the first day of attacks. Both countries continued to pound Iran, conducting hundreds of airstrikes across the country overnight and on Sunday. Despite the apparent loss of a significant portion of its senior military and political leadership, Iran did not slow its retaliation on Sunday, bombing targets in the Gulf and unleashing waves of ballistic missiles towards Israel. Continue reading...

title_original: Gulf states under attack: What was targeted in Iranian strikes?
content_original: Gulf states are witnessing unprecedented scenes at airports, military facilities, and hotels from Oman to Kuwait amid waves of Iranian missile and drone strikes. Iran's response has extended from retaliatory strikes on US military bases in the Gulf to targeting civilian areas, as far away as Duqm in central Oman . The New Arab takes a look at the main places hit in the Gulf over the past 24 hours. UAE The UAE has been the country hardest hit in the Gulf by Iran's retaliatory strikes, likely due to Abu Dhabi's close ties with Israel and antagonistic relationship with Tehran. UAE authorities announced on Sunday that three people have died and 58 injured in the country since start of Iran strikes. Saturday saw scenes of tourists and residents fleeing falling missile and debris as hotels, airports and other civilian infrastructure were targeted in 137 attempted missile and 209 drone strikes across the country. Dubai International Airport was damaged in an attack on Saturday, causing four i...
title_en: Iranian missile and drone strikes target Gulf states, causing casualties and damage

According to the Egyptian presidency, Al-Sisi discussed the latest developments with Sultan Haitham, strongly condemning the targeting of Duqm Port earlier in the day. He reiterated Egypt’s long-standing position that regional crises must be resolved through dialogue and peaceful means, warning of the grave consequences of further escalation and the risk of the region descending into wider instability. title_original: 'A terrible day,' says Israel community shaken by deadly Iranian strike
content_original: Ilana Malka doesn't know if her three great-nephews are alive after an Iranian missile struck a shelter in her city of Beit Shemesh, in central Israel, leaving a crater in the ground and a void in the community. The building that housed the shelter was blown to bits by the hit, sending chunks of concrete large enough to destroy cars 50 metres (yards) away. The missile hit the public protection centre while its door was still open to allow people in, Malka and other residents said, killing at least 9, injuring 46, and leaving 11 missing, according to the police.
title_en: Israeli Community Shaken by Deadly Iranian Missile Strike
summary_en: An Iranian ballistic missile struck a public shelter in Beit Shemesh, central Israel, killing at least 9 people, injuring 46, with 11 still missing. The missile hit while the shelter door was open to allow civilians in, destroying the building and sending concrete chunks 50 meters away. This strike represents a significant Iranian attack on Israeli civilian infrastructure, marking an escalation in the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict connected to the broader US-Iran hostilities.

classification.brief: Iranian ballistic missile struck a civilian shelter in Beit Shemesh, killing at least 9 people and injuring 46 in a major direct Iranian attack on Israeli civilians.

By late morning on Saturday, the beach clubs on Palm Jumeirah were already at capacity. Along the waterfront promenade, running clubs gathered beneath the towers, filming their warm-ups before setting off in neat formation. On Instagram, the city appeared untouched: blue skies, a flat sea and the steady churn of shoppers inside the Dubai Mall. Across the Gulf, however, the largest regional war since the 2003 invasion of Iraq was intensifying. Continue reading...

title_original: Enemy within: Counterterrorism experts fear sleeper cells could be poised inside USto
content_original: American counterterrorism agencies are quietly monitoring suspected sleeper cells on U.S. soil in the wake of joint U.S.–Israel strikes on Iran , stepping up surveillance amid heightened fears of possible retaliation from Iran-linked operatives or sympathizers. Federal and local law enforcement have also boosted on-the-ground security in major U.S. cities as part of a precautionary posture, even though no specific, credible threats have been publicly identified. The move comes on the heels of a Saturday morning operation, dubbed Operation Epic Fury, that resulted in the death of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and several senior Iranian officials in a coordinated U.S.–Israeli military campaign. US, ISRAEL STRIKE IRAN: AYATOLLAH KHAMENEI, 40 TOP IRANIAN LEADERS KILLED Security experts warn that the fallout could extend beyond the Middle East. title_original: What we know about the US-Israeli killing of Ali Khamenei
content_original: SupremeLeader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei , 86, who ruled Iran for more than three decades, was killed on 28 February 2026 in a coordinated US-Israeli strike targeting Iran's senior leadership. Tehran's state media confirmed his death hours later and declared a 40-day national mourning period, amid waves of strikes on Iran by Israel and the US. The killing marks the most consequential decapitation strike against the Islamic Republic since its founding in 1979 and has triggered immediate retaliation and a formal constitutional transition inside Iran. What happened? The strike took place during the opening phase of a broader US-Israeli military campaign targeting Iranian military and strategic sites. Within hours of the initial wave, Israeli and US officials publicly stated that Khamenei had been killed. After hours of denials, Iranian authorities later confirmed his death on state television. The Supreme National Security Council formally acknowledged the assassination and announced 40 days ...

title_original: 📝 Морская   война или френдли фаер? 📝 Сегодня у побережья Омана нападению подвергся  нефтяной танкер Skylight , шедши...
content_original: 📝 Морская война или френдли фаер? 📝 Сегодня у побережья Омана нападению подвергся нефтяной танкер Skylight , шедший под флагом Палау . Об том сообщили в Центре морской безопасности страны. В ходе атаки дроном-камикадзе пострадали четыре человека, весь экипаж был эвакуирован. Находились же там 15 индийцев и еще пять граждан Ирана. На данный момент корабль горит. ©️ Но самая интересная деталь: в нападении обвинили иранцев, при этом танкер находится под санкциями Министерства финансов США за... перевозку иранских нефтепродуктов. ❓ Казалось бы, факт наводит на противоречие. Как иранцы могли ударить по цели, которую американцы считают частью теневого флота Исламской республики? 🖍 Однако важно понимать, что Ормузский пролив — такая же зона боя. Танкер мог запросто попасть под огонь, так как одновременно шел удар беспилотниками по порту Дукум в Омане. 🚩 В условиях войны и дестабилизации обстановки в стране у иранцев нет возможности уточнять статус контрабандных танкеров. Тем более, что на кор...

title_original: Travellers say some Iranians held up at Turkish land border
content_original: (Adds story links) KAPIKOY, Turkey , March 1 (Reuters) - Some Iranian citizens are being stopped from crossing into Turkey at Iran's northwestern Khoy land border gate, according to three foreign passport-holders who crossed on Sunday and some local Turkish media. A day after the United States and Israel launched attacks on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other senior officials, one of the travellers told Reuters there were increasing numbers of those waiting to cross into Turkey's eastern Van province.

summary_en: Following U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other senior officials, some Iranian citizens are being prevented from crossing into Turkey at the northwestern Khoy border gate. classification.location: Khoy border gate, Turkey-Iran border, northwestern Iran/Eastern Van province Turkey

title_original: Iran: Thousands take to streets to mourn Ali Khamenei's killing
content_original: Iran: Thousands take to streets to mourn Ali Khamenei's killing Submitted by MEE staff on Sun, 03/01/2026 - 10:21 Tearful mourners gather at shrines and in city squares after state media confirms US-Israeli killing of Iranian supreme leader People mourn Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, killed in joint US-Israeli strikes, at a square in Tehran on 1 March 2026 (AFP/Atta Kenare) Off Thousands of mourners have gathered across Iran to mourn and protest following the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.  Khamenei, who had been the supreme leader since 1989, was killed in the opening salvo of US and Israeli attacks on Saturday morning.  His death was confirmed by Iranian state media in the early hours of Sunday.  Mourners gather in Enghelab Square in Tehran, many dressed in black, waving Iranian flags and holding photos of Khamenei.  According to AFP, they chanted slogans including “Death to America” and called for retaliation against Israel and the US. title_original: Israeli army says 'unaware' of strike on school that killed at least 115
content_original: Israeli army says 'unaware' of strike on school that killed at least 115 The Israeli military said Sunday it was "unaware" of a US or Israeli air strike on a school that left at least 115 dead in southern Iran. "At this stage, we have no knowledge of an Israeli or US airstrike at that location... We operate with extreme precision," military spokesman Lieutenant Colonel Nadav Shoshani said at a press briefing. State media reported at least 115 killed in the air strike that destroyed Shajareh Tayyebeh school in the city of Minab, in Hormozgan province.

content_original: Iranians were experiencing a mix of shock, grief and joy after the death of supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as Israeli and US strikes extended into a second day Sunday. The attacks unleashed on Saturday killed Khamenei and top military leaders and prompted Iranian authorities to retaliate with strikes on Israel and across the Gulf. At the first reports of Khamenei's death, many Iranians erupted into cheers from apartment buildings in the capital Tehran while others blared car horns and blasted music in the streets.

title_original: Oil tanker hit off Oman coast after drones strike Duqm port
content_original: A Palau-flagged oil tanker was hit off Oman's Musandam peninsula on Sunday injuring four people, the country's maritime security centre said, following drone strikes on the Gulf country's commercial port of Duqm . The incidents mark the first time targets in or near Oman have been hit following a wave of retaliatory strikes by Tehran on Gulf states after joint U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran that have plunged the region into a new war. The 20-person crew of the Skylight tanker was evacuated after the attack, which occurred about 5 nautical miles north of Musandam's Khasab Port, the Oman Maritime Security Centre said in a post on X . The centre did not specify what hit the tanker. Initial information indicated injuries of varying severity to four of the crew, composed of 15 Indian and five Iranian citizens, it added. Oman's Musandam peninsula shares control of the Strait of Hormuz with Iran, a vital strategic chokepoint through which roughly a fifth of global oil consumption flows. Earlier...

The attack on the tanker and port highlights the widening scope of the conflict and potential threats to maritime security in the Gulf region.

title_original: US military says three of its service members killed in Iran operation
content_original: WASHINGTON, March 1 (Reuters) - Three U.S. service members have been killed in action as part of U.S. military operations against Iran, the U.S. Central Command said in a statement on Sunday. Five others were seriously wounded as part of Operation Epic Fury, it said. (Reporting by Doina Chiacu; Editing by Paul Simao)
title_en: Three US service members killed in Iran operation
summary_en: Three US service members were killed and five others seriously wounded during Operation Epic Fury against Iran, as reported by US Central Command. This highlights the ongoing direct military engagement between the US and Iran, with casualties impacting US forces.

classification.brief: Three US service members were killed and five wounded in Operation Epic Fury, indicating direct military engagement between the US and Iran.

title_original: Three tankers damaged in Gulf as US-Iran conflict escalates
content_original: By Yousef Saba, Jaidaa Taha and Jonathan Saul DUBAI/LONDON, March 1 (Reuters) - At least three tankers were damaged off the Gulf coast after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran triggered Iranian retaliation that put merchant ships at risk of collateral damage, shipping sources and officials said on Sunday. Risks to commercial shipping have surged in the past 24 hours, with more than 200 vessels including oil and liquefied gas tankers dropping anchor around the Strait of Hormuz and surrounding waters, shipping data showed on Sunday.
title_en: Three tankers damaged in Gulf amid US-Iran conflict escalation
summary_en: At least three tankers were damaged off the Gulf coast after US and Israeli strikes on Iran prompted Iranian retaliation, posing risks to commercial shipping. Over 200 vessels, including oil and liquefied gas tankers, have anchored near the Strait of Hormuz and surrounding waters, highlighting the increased risks to maritime traffic in the region.

classification.location: Gulf of Oman, Strait of Hormuz
classification.brief: Three tankers were damaged in a naval incident amid escalating US-Iran tensions, with US and Israeli strikes prompting Iranian retaliation, impacting over 200 commercial vessels in the strategic waterway.

title_original: Trump’s current war on Iran picks up where a longstanding enmity left off
content_original: The mutual resentments that have fueled tensions between the US and Iran have simmered for nearly half a century For millions of younger Americans, the sudden explosion of Iran onto the national political stage and consciousness may seem like a bolt from the blue. Yet for older generations and those with deeper historical awareness, Donald Trump ’s announcement on Saturday of strikes against a distant foe is more like the outcome of a collision long foretold. Continue reading...
title_en: Trump’s current war on Iran picks up where longstanding enmity left off
summary_en: The article discusses the historical tensions between the US and Iran, culminating in the recent military strikes announced by Donald Trump. This signifies an escalation in the ongoing conflict, reflecting longstanding animosity and strategic confrontations between the two nations.

classification.brief: The article describes a significant escalation in the US-Iran conflict, with Donald Trump announcing military strikes against Iran, marking a direct engagement between the two nations.

summary_en: Saudi Arabia has strongly condemned Iran's aggression against Oman, emphasizing full solidarity with Oman. This highlights escalating regional tensions as Iran reportedly violated Oman's sovereignty, potentially indicating a new front in the ongoing conflict.

classification.brief: Iran's aggression against Oman, condemned by Saudi Arabia, marks a significant escalation in regional tensions and could indicate a new front in the Iran-US conflict.

title_original: Oman port targeted by Iranian drones: state media
content_original: Oman port targeted by Iranian drones: state media A port in Oman was targeted by Iranian drones, state media said on Sunday. The country, which had been hosting Iran-US talks and so far had avoided Iranian retaliation,  saw a drone attack that injured one person at the port of Duqm , while an oil tanker was also targeted off its coast.
title_en: Iranian drones target Omani port
summary_en: Iranian drones attacked the port of Duqm in Oman, injuring one person and targeting an oil tanker off the coast. This incident is significant as Oman had previously hosted Iran-US talks and avoided direct Iranian retaliation until now. The attack demonstrates Iran's willingness to target neutral countries in the region amid ongoing hostilities.

classification.brief: Iran launched a drone attack on Oman's Duqm port and an oil tanker, injuring one person, marking a significant escalation by targeting a neutral country that previously hosted Iran-US talks.

title_original: US-sanctioned oil tanker hit off Oman coast
content_original: March 1 (Reuters) - A Palau-flagged oil tanker under U.S. sanctions was hit on Sunday off Oman's Musandam peninsula, injuring four people, the country's maritime security centre said, without specifying what hit the vessel. The attack followed earlier drone strikes elsewhere in the Gulf country, at the commercial port of Duqm on the Arabian Sea. The incidents mark the first time targets in or near Oman have been hit following a wave of retaliatory strikes by Tehran on Gulf states after joint U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran that have plunged the region into a new war.
title_en: US-sanctioned oil tanker attacked off Oman coast
summary_en: An oil tanker under US sanctions was struck near the Musandam peninsula in Oman, injuring four people. The incident follows earlier drone strikes at the Duqm port in Oman, marking the first attacks in or near Oman amid escalating regional tensions from US-Israeli strikes on Iran and Tehran's retaliatory attacks on Gulf states.

classification.brief: An oil tanker under US sanctions was attacked near Oman, marking an escalation in regional tensions amid ongoing US-Iran hostilities.

title_original: Missile debris, panic buying jolt Dubai's aura of calm after Iran attack
content_original: By Raghed Waked, Amr Alfiky, Timour Azhari and Rula Rouhana DUBAI, March 1 (Reuters) - As Iran fired a barrage of drones and missiles at Gulf States in retaliation for U.S. and Israeli strikes, some in Dubai ran for cover and others froze at windows, watching the sky as if trying to make sense of something that had seemed impossible.
title_en: Missile debris, panic buying jolt Dubai's calm after Iran attack
summary_en: Iran's retaliatory missile and drone attacks on Gulf States have caused panic and disrupted the sense of calm in Dubai, with residents seeking cover and observing the skies in fear. The incident highlights the regional instability and potential risks to Gulf cities amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict. This event underscores the broader strategic implications for US allies and commercial hubs in the region.

classification.brief: Iran's retaliatory missile and drone attacks on Gulf States have heightened regional tensions and caused panic in Dubai amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

title_original: A new war
content_original: UNLESS there is an immediate diplomatic breakthrough, the joint Israeli-American aggression against Iran launched on Saturday morning has the potential to create a maelstrom across the Middle East and beyond. Already hostilities have spread, as the Iranians, along with lobbing missiles at Israel, have also hit American military facilities in several Gulf states, just as they threatened they would in the run-up to this unfolding disaster. For Iran’s rulers, this is the third time they have been double-crossed by US President Donald Trump. The first time was when Mr Trump withdrew from the JCPOA in 2018, the second when America joined Israel in the 12-day war last year. On Thursday, after talks in Geneva, it had seemed that things might be moving towards a resolution, particularly on the nuclear issue, with the Omani foreign minister saying that “peace … is within our reach”. But clearly, the US and Israel had other ideas. Mr Trump has referred to himself as a man of peace , claiming tha...
title_en: Joint Israeli-American Aggression Against Iran Escalates Middle East Conflict
summary_en: Iran has retaliated against US military facilities in several Gulf states following joint Israeli-American aggression. This escalation has the potential to create widespread conflict across the Middle East. The hostilities mark the third time Iran feels betrayed by US actions, following the JCPOA withdrawal and last year's conflict.

classification.brief: Iran retaliates against US military facilities in Gulf states following joint Israeli-American aggression, escalating regional conflict.

title_original: Recap
content_original: Recap Good morning, Middle East Eye readers. If you’re just joining us, here’s a quick recap of the latest developments across the region: The US and Israel launched air strikes on Iran on Saturday shortly after 9:30am local time (6am GMT). Iranian state TV announced the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.  Iran’s Red Crescent Society says US and Israeli attacks have struck 24 provinces across the country, leaving 201 people dead and 747 wounded. Hamas mourns "heinous" killing of Khamenei, while Iran's president brands it "declaration of war against Muslims." At least 115 killed, mostly young girls, after a deadly strike on a school in southern Iran. Six killed in Pakistan as protestors storm US Consulate in Karachi after Khamenei assassination Iran targets US military bases in the region, with strikes reported in Riyadh, Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Manama, Doha and Kuwait. Blasts were also heard in the Jordanian capital, Amman.
title_en: US and Israel launch air strikes on Iran; Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei reported dead
summary_en: The US and Israel conducted air strikes on Iran, resulting in significant casualties and damage across 24 provinces, with 201 people killed and 747 wounded. The strikes occurred shortly after 9:30 am local time. Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was reported dead by Iranian state TV, escalating regional tensions and prompting strong condemnation from Iranian leadership.

classification.brief: US and Israel launched coordinated airstrikes on Iran, causing widespread damage and casualties, including the reported death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

title_original: Trump bit off more than he can chew with Iran – ex-Pentagon analyst
content_original: The US is doing Israel’s bidding with strikes on Iran that are unlikely to trigger regime change, Michael Maloof has told RT The US-Israeli strikes on Iran are unlikely to trigger regime change and risk escalating into a wider geopolitical confrontation, former Pentagon security policy analyst Michael Maloof has told RT. Washington and West Jerusalem launched what they described as a “preemptive” attack on the Islamic Republic after nuclear talks failed to produce a breakthrough, prompting retaliation from Iran. Tehran responded with missile and drone strikes targeting Israel and US military bases across the region. In an interview with RT on Saturday, Maloof said the timing of the attack had likely been finalized during Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s visit to Mar-a-Lago on February 12, despite President Donald Trump publicly insisting that negotiations with Tehran were ongoing. “The United States has always done Israel’s bidding. Netanyahu ba...
title_en: Trump's Iran strategy criticized by ex-Pentagon analyst
summary_en: Former Pentagon security policy analyst Michael Maloof criticized the US for conducting strikes on Iran at Israel's behest, arguing that these attacks are unlikely to lead to regime change and risk escalating into a broader geopolitical conflict. Iran retaliated with missile and drone strikes targeting US and Israeli military bases in the region after a failed nuclear deal.

classification.brief: The article discusses escalating US-Iran tensions, with US strikes on Iran at Israel's urging and Iranian retaliatory missile and drone strikes, indicating a high-severity conflict with potential for broader escalation.

title_original: Six killed as protestors storm US Consulate in Karachi after Khamenei assassination
content_original: Six killed as protestors storm US Consulate in Karachi after Khamenei assassination Six people were killed and several others were wounded during a demonstration near the US Consulate in Karachi, Pakistan, the Edhi rescue service said. Protesters tried to storm the diplomatic compound, and police used force to disperse the crowd, injuring multiple people, according to officials. Demonstrators stormed the US Consulate in Pakistan's Karachi amid a surge in tensions, sparked by anger over recent US and Israeli attacks on Iran, alongside the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei. Video footage showed security forces opening fire as angry demonstrators set fire to the guardhouse.  Reports: 10+ killed after soldiers open fire onpeople protesting at US consulate in Karachi pic.twitter.com/6m95XNqm59 — Press TV 🔻 (@PressTV) March 1, 2026
title_en: Six killed as protestors storm US Consulate in Karachi after Khamenei assassination
summary_en: Protestors in Karachi, Pakistan, stormed the US Consulate following the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei and recent US and Israeli attacks on Iran, resulting in six deaths and multiple injuries. The incident highlights escalating regional tensions and anti-US sentiment due to the ongoing conflict. The clash underscores potential risks to US diplomatic facilities in the region amid the conflict's fallout.

classification.brief: Protests in Karachi escalate into violence at the US Consulate following the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei, resulting in six deaths and highlighting regional tensions.

title_original: 📝 Дубайские огни, иранские ракеты 📝 В ночь с 28 февраля на 1 марта Иран нанёс масштабный ракетно-дроновый удар по объ...
content_original: 📝 Дубайские огни, иранские ракеты 📝 В ночь с 28 февраля на 1 марта Иран нанёс масштабный ракетно-дроновый удар по объектам Объединённых Арабских Эмиратов в рамках ответной кампании на совместную операцию США и Израиля «Epic Fury». По данным Министерства обороны ОАЭ, Иран выпустил более 130 баллистических ракет и порядка 200 ударных и барражирующих дронов — подавляющее большинство, по заявлениям местного МО, было якобы перехвачено эмиратской системой ПВО при поддержке союзников. 🔻 Тем не менее заметная часть ракет и дронов до целей прорвалась: ▪️ Повреждения получили Дубайский международный аэропорт и внешние конструкции отеля Burj Al Arab, в разных районах Дубая и Абу-Даби зафиксированы пожары и материальный ущерб от обломков перехваченных ракет. Один человек погиб в Абу-Даби, ещё несколько получили ранения. ▪️ Оба крупнейших аэропорта ОАЭ временно прекращали работу; параллельно было закрыто воздушное пространство над Ираком, Кувейтом, Бахрейном, Катаром, что вызвало масштабные сбои в ...
title_en: Iran launches large-scale missile and drone attack on UAE in response to US-Israel 'Epic Fury' operation
summary_en: Iran launched a major missile and drone attack targeting the UAE on the night of February 28 to March 1, in retaliation for the US-Israel 'Epic Fury' operation. Despite UAE claims that most projectiles were intercepted, significant damage was reported to Dubai International Airport and the Burj Al Arab hotel, indicating a partial breakthrough of Iranian munitions.

classification.brief: Iran launched a major missile and drone attack on the UAE in retaliation for the US-Israel 'Epic Fury' operation, causing significant damage to infrastructure.

title_original: Khamenei — the supreme leader who held ultimate control over Iran’s political, military, religious institutions
content_original: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who ruled he Islamic republic for over 30 years, has been killed in a an strike by US and Israel — the two countries that had been calling for a regime change in Tehran. Khamenei, 86, became Iran’s highest authority in 1989, following the death of the Islamic Republic’s founder, Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini. He remained in power after overcoming 1999 student demonstrations, 2009 mass protests sparked by disputed presidential elections and 2019 demonstrations that were brutally suppressed. He also survived the 2022-2023 “Woman, Life, Freedom” movement sparked by the death in custody of Iranian Kurdish woman Mahsa Amini, who had been arrested for allegedly violating the strict dress code for women. As supreme leader, he held ultimate control over Iran’s political, military and religious institutions, shaping domestic policy and guiding foreign relations. An article published in Al Jazeera said “critical to Khamenei’s power is the loyalty of tw...

title_original: 📝 Шиитский форпост 📝 снова пострадал Бахрейн Продолжает прилетать и по Бахрейну. На самом деле,  катарские шейхи  ещё...
content_original: 📝 Шиитский форпост 📝 снова пострадал Бахрейн Продолжает прилетать и по Бахрейну. На самом деле, катарские шейхи ещё сравнительно легко отделались : в ОАЭ всё горят отели и аэропорт, а в маленьком шиитском государстве, увы, гражданские высотки тоже попали под раздачу. В очередной раз прилетело на территорию отеля Crowne Plaza Manama в дипломатическом квартале — аккурат в паре кварталов от штаба 5-го флота ВМС США . На юге Манамы начались демонстрации с чёрным знаменем месте на фоне сообщений о гибели Али Хаменеи . Картинка, конечно, красивая, но и только. 📌 В Сети появилась, конечно, версия, что иранцы попытаются раздуть протесты на территории Бахрейна ещё сильнее, дабы повлиять на обстановку в стране и привести правильных людей к власти, но такими темпами население Бахрейна и местные шейхи к Ирану, наоборот, станут испытывать ещё больше антипатии. #Иран #Катар #США 🧨 @rybar_mena — о ближневосточном хаосе с любовью 💸 Поддержать нас

Правоверные шииты вместе с религиозными фанатиками занялись привычным делом: попытками прорваться в Зелёную зону Багдада к посольству США . 📌 В Ираке находится одно из самых больших американских посольств в мире: во время войны против ИГ близлежащая территория была переоборудована в полноценную военную базу, предназначенную для развертывания немаленького контингента ВС США и Международной коалиции. На территории спокойно могут умещаться несколько вертолётов и конвертопланов. Протесты и митинги наблюдаются не только в Багдаде : бастует Басра , в священных шиитских городах траур. Правоохранительные органы в столице пытаются подавить протесты и продемонстрировать приверженность правопорядку. До американского посольства протестующие за последние года смогли добраться только в 2020 году: тогда разбили КПП и стащили журнал учёта посетит...

content_original: 📝 Трансфер во время войны 📝 Верховный лидер Ирана убит: что дальше? Среди ночи в Иране официально подтвердили гибель Верховного лидера Ирана Али Хаменеи . Секретарь Совета национальной безопасности Ирана Али Лариджани объявил о формировании Руководящего совета , который возьмёт на себя бразды правления страной на ближайшее время. На протяжении истории Иран переживал «куда худшие времена» — примерно так охарактеризовал произошедшее де-факто глава Ирана. Президент Масуд Пезешкиан пообещал стереть «американо-сионистских агрессоров». Спикер парламента Ирана Мохаммад Багер Галибаф пообещал разрушительные удары и в очередной раз подчеркнул, что «переступившие красную черту заплатят высокую цену». Планы на случай гибели Али Хаменеи были проработаны заранее. В Иране наблюдается колоссальная поддержка войны до победного конца: по улицам проходят многочисленные митинги и демонстрации. title_original: Iran announces attacks on Israel, 27 US bases in Middle East
content_original: Iran announces attacks on Israel, 27 US bases in Middle East The IRGC on Sunday said that it is carrying out a sixth wave of attacks in retaliation for the US and Israeli bombardment of Iran, state media reported.  The report said that the IRGC has launched “extensive missile and drone” attacks on Israel and US military bases in the region. It said that the Israeli Tel Nof airbase, the Israeli army’s command headquarters at HaKirya in Tel Aviv, and a large defence industrial complex in the same city, were attacked, in addition to 27 US bases in the region.  It added that Iranian forces will “implement a different and harsh step of revenge, with successive, regrettable slaps”.

These attacks signify a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict.

As crowds gathered in Tehran, explosions rang out and the Israeli military announced that it was again striking targets in the heart of the city -- as more blasts were heard in Jerusalem, Riyadh, Dubai, Doha and Manama.

title_original: Reuters: Multiple explosions heard in Dubai City
content_original: Reuters: Multiple explosions heard in Dubai City Several loud bangs were heard in Dubai City on Sunday, Reuters news agency has reported.  Earlier, the Dubai Media Office said that a fire at one of the berths of the Jebel Ali Port occurred after it was hit by debris from an “aerial interception”. The office said that Dubai’s Civil Defence teams are “continuing their efforts to fully extinguish the fire” at the commercial port, which is the largest in the Middle East. Jebel Ali Port is located just south of Palm Jumeirah, where an explosion was reported earlier. A missile strike linked to Iran also triggered a fire at Dubai’s Burj Al Arab, one of the city’s most recognisable landmarks.
title_en: Explosions and fire reported at Jebel Ali Port in Dubai after aerial interception
summary_en: Explosions and a subsequent fire were reported at Jebel Ali Port in Dubai after debris from an 'aerial interception' hit the area. The incident occurred amidst ongoing regional tensions, with potential links to recent missile activity, highlighting the vulnerability of critical infrastructure in the UAE.

classification.brief: An aerial interception over Dubai resulted in explosions and fire at Jebel Ali Port, indicating a potential Houthi drone strike amid regional tensions.

title_original: Waves of blasts heard over Dubai, Doha for second day, witnesses say
content_original: By Maha El Dahan and Andrew Mills DUBAI, March 1 (Reuters) - Loud blasts were heard in Dubai and the Qatari capital Doha for the second day on Sunday, witnesses said, as Iran's retaliatory strikes on neighbouring Gulf states in response to U.S. and Israeli strikes on the Islamic Republic widened. Iran had said it would target U.S. bases in the region but it has hit a range of other targets across Gulf cities. Two people were injured in Dubai after shrapnel from drones fell over two houses when they were intercepted, aDubai Media office statement said.
title_en: Waves of blasts heard over Dubai and Doha for second day as Iran widens retaliatory strikes
summary_en: Iran has expanded its retaliatory strikes to include Dubai and Doha, hitting targets in Gulf cities for the second day. This escalation indicates Iran's intent to target US interests and allied Gulf states amid ongoing conflict with the US and Israel.

title_original: Two dead as Iran launches waves of strikes on wealthy Gulf
content_original: Explosions rocked cities across the Gulf on Saturday, killing two people in Abu Dhabi, while smoke and flames rose from Dubai landmark The Palm as Iran launched waves of attacks in retaliation for US and Israeli strikes . The attacks hit airports in Abu Dhabi, Dubai and Kuwait , as well as Gulf military bases and residential areas, raising fears of a wider conflict and rattling a region long seen as a haven of peace and security. Across the UAE , Iran fired 137 missiles and 209 drones at the territory, the country's defence ministry said, as projectiles streaked across the skies of every Gulf state but Oman, a mediator in the recent US-Iran talks. The UAE defence ministry said most of the missiles and drones were intercepted but at Abu Dhabi's Zayed International Airport officials said at least one person was killed and seven wounded in an "incident". Earlier, falling debris killed a Pakistani civilian in Abu Dhabi, the United Arab Emirates' capital, officials said. At Dubai Internatio...

The attacks resulted in two deaths in Abu Dhabi and caused significant damage to infrastructure, escalating regional tensions and signaling a potential widening of the conflict. The UAE reported that most projectiles were intercepted, but the scale and coordination of the assault highlight Iran's capability to strike deep into Gulf states.

classification.brief: Iran launched a major retaliatory attack using 137 missiles and 209 drones on Gulf cities, causing two deaths in Abu Dhabi and significant infrastructure damage.

Dubai airport, the world's busiest for international traffic, and Jebel Ali seaport also suffered damage as the unprecedented attacks hit some of the city's major landmarks and revenue-earners. Two witnesses on the Palm - an upscale, palm tree-shaped residential and leisure development - heard an explosion and saw smoke rising from a five-star hotel. The Dubai media office confirmed an "incident" in a building on the Palm Jumeirah caused a fire and injured four people. Hours later, a second blast exploded close to the same building, after what looked like a drone plunged from above, another witness saw. The United Arab Emirates said 137 missiles and 209 drones were fired at its territory by Iran, most of them intercepted by defence systems. Debris from an intercepted drone sparked a fir...

title_original: Fire at Jebel Ali port in Dubai after interception of fallen debris, officials say
content_original: Fire at Jebel Ali port in Dubai after interception of fallen debris, officials say The Dubai Media Office on Saturday said that a fire at one of the berths of the Jebel Ali Port occurred after it was hit by debris from an “aerial interception”. The office said that Dubai’s Civil Defence teams are “continuing their efforts to fully extinguish the fire” at the commercial port, which is the largest in the Middle East. Jebel Ali Port is located just south of Palm Jumeirah, where an explosion was reported earlier. A missile strike linked to Iran also triggered a fire at Dubai’s Burj Al Arab, one of the city’s most recognisable landmarks. Dubai authorities confirm that debris resulting from an aerial interception caused a fire at one of the berths at Jebel Ali Port. Dubai Civil Defense teams responded immediately and are continuing their efforts to fully extinguish the fire. No injuries have been reported.… — Dubai Media Office (@DXBMediaOffice) February 28, 2026
title_en: Fire at Jebel Ali port in Dubai after interception of fallen debris
summary_en: A fire broke out at Jebel Ali Port in Dubai after debris from an aerial interception hit the area. The incident occurred near Palm Jumeirah, where an explosion was previously reported, suggesting potential missile or drone activity linked to the ongoing conflict. The fire's proximity to a major commercial port in the Middle East raises concerns about potential disruptions to regional trade and maritime operations.

classification.brief: A fire at Jebel Ali Port in Dubai, caused by intercepted debris, indicates a drone strike or missile interception linked to the Iran-US conflict.

title_original: Unauthorized war or justified action? US Congress split on Iran strikes
content_original: Republicans broadly support President Donald Trump while Democrats criticize his “major combat operation” as an undeclared war US President Donald Trump’s unilateral decision to launch strikes against Iran has met a mixed response in Congress, as lawmakers are split over the issue, mostly along party lines. Democrats in Congress are now seeking to stop the campaign under the 1973 War Powers Act. Washington and West Jerusalem launched the attack on Saturday, with Trump openly stating that the operation was aimed at bringing about regime change in Iran to eliminate “imminent threats” to the American people. The attacks killed over 200 people, according to the Iranian Red Crescent Society. Tehran responded by launching retaliatory strikes against targets in Israel as well as US bases in the region. READ MORE: US-Israeli attack on Iran plunges Middle East into chaos: LIVE UPDATES, VIDEOS, PHOTOS Iran called the attack a blatant violation of its sovereignty. Russia and Chi...

The attacks, which Trump claims aim to eliminate 'imminent threats' and bring regime change in Iran, have resulted in over 200 casualties according to Iranian sources. This escalation highlights the deepening political divide in the US regarding the ongoing conflict with Iran.

classification.brief: US Congress is divided over President Trump's unilateral decision to launch strikes against Iran, with Republicans supporting the action and Democrats seeking to halt it under the War Powers Act.

title_original: US women's basketball player in Israel shares panic in footage of Iranian counterattack on Jerusalem
content_original: Former NCAA and Team USA women's basketball player Destiny Littleton shared footage Saturday of her experience fleeing Iranian counterstrikes in Israel. Her documentation concluded with a panicked scene of her and other civilians shouting in fear as drones flew overhead. Littleton, who won a national championship at South Carolina under coach Dawn Staley in 2022, and a gold medal for the U.S. in the 2017 FIBA 3x3 U18 World Cup, currently plays for Hapoel Jerusalem in Israel’s top division. She posted footage on her Instagram Saturday updating followers as she fled to a local bomb shelter when Iranian counterattacks began to strike Israel. After the U.S. and Israel carried out a round of military strikes on Iran, the country responded with ballistic missiles and drones targeting cities including Tel Aviv, Haifa, and Jerusalem. The conservative critics pointed to potential risks for Republicans after the president campaigned in 2024 on a promise to focus on the economy and not start wars. Public opinion polls show voters are increasingly disillusioned with Trump's handling of the economy. Jack Posobiec, a right-wing commentator and influencer, pointed to a warning last year from the late conservative activist Charlie Kirk before he was assassinated in September. "Charlie Kirk told us all that the younger generation of Americans are far more interested in domestic policy than pursuing international conflicts and we can’t forget that in a midterm year," Posobiec posted on X. title_original: Drone Attacks Target Military Sites in Southern Iraq
content_original: By John Lee. Iraqi air defences have intercepted and shot down nine drones targeting military sites in Dhi Qar and Basra provinces, according to updates from the Joint Operations Command . Four drones were downed after attempting to strike a military location inside Imam Ali Air Base in Dhi Qar province. Two were intercepted at 22:50, with no casualties or material losses reported. In separate incidents, five additional drones were detected and destroyed while attempting to target military sites in Basra province. One earlier attempt to strike a military location in Basra resulted in a soldier being injured. In total, nine drones were intercepted across the reported incidents. Authorities confirmed that, aside from the one injured soldier, no further human or material losses were recorded. Investigations have been launched in line with established procedures. Separately, Jurf Al-Nasr in northern Babil province was subjected to two air strikes at 19:25. The authorities said they are clo...

title_original: Iran's Ali Khamenei, who based iron rule on fiery hostility to US and Israel, dies at 86
content_original: By Parisa Hafezi WASHINGTON, Feb. 28 (Reuters) - The 36-year rule of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei built Iran into a powerful anti-U.S. force, spreading its military sway across the Middle East, while using an iron fist to crush repeated unrest at home. He was killed on Saturday, aged 86, Iranian state media announced, in air strikes by Israel and the U.S. that pulverised his central Tehran compound, after decades of efforts to resolve the dispute over Iran's nuclear program diplomatically failed.

content_original: Trump says reports of Khamenei’s death ‘we feel are correct’ US President Donald Trump has said he believes reports that Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in air strikes are accurate, though he stopped short of confirming the claim outright. “We feel that that is a correct story,” Trump told NBC News. Speaking separately to ABC News, he added: “I don't want to say anything definitively until I see things but we believe he is. And much of their leaders are gone.” His remarks follow earlier comments from Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who said there were “many signs” that Khamenei had been killed in a strike on his compound during the joint US-Israeli campaign. Iranian media outlets have denied the reports.

title_original: 'Dubai's nightmare': Iranian strikes shatter calm of UAE business hub
content_original: 'Dubai's nightmare': Iranian strikes shatter calm of UAE business hub Iran  unleashed a furious missile and drone assault across the Arabian Gulf on Saturday in response to a surprise  US-Israeli attack , but one city in particular appeared to bear the  brunt  of it: Dubai. The Gulf emirate, one of seven in the  UAE , is the region’s good-time capital, where lucrative business deals can be sealed and celebrated. On Saturday, it was engulfed in billowing smoke and flames as missiles and drones rained down on it. The strikes have more than just geopolitical significance, denting Dubai's reputation for being an oasis of calm and commerce in the Middle East. Dubai has benefited potentially more than any other city in the world from the post-Covid boom of soaring asset prices, cryptocurrency, and remote work. Capitalising on its low tax rate and smooth bureaucracy, Dubai has become a magnet for London bankers and American “finance bros." Its financial institutions h...

title_original: 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇮🇷   On the Iran-Israel War:  /CIG/  #commentary Main takeaway is that the Iranian response was timely, instead...
content_original: 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 On the Iran-Israel War: /CIG/ #commentary Main takeaway is that the Iranian response was timely, instead of the 24-48 hour wait last time. Pretty decent day one for Iran. Expected performance by the US et al. Global shipping might be the main casualty here. Unknown what will happen to oil & gas. The main damage caused by Iran et al in recent years was by shutting down the Suez. The US also appears to be shifting towards lower-cost kamikaze drones, taking a page out of Iran's book. Iran focused on radars and anything which aids interceptions. This is better than hitting symbolic buildings like last time. They're taking taking big hits and their leadership may or may not be dead, but they prepared for decentralized military coordination. So it's like a unit by unit basis of commanders making self authorized decisions. Main US military impact is that this will functionally use up the remainder of munitions that would have been used on China. Also the US is not invading Iran shock...

The US is also noted to be adopting Iran's kamikaze drone tactics, reflecting a shift in military strategy.

title_original: US, Israel defend strikes at UN as Iran alleges 'war crime'
content_original: The United States and Israel on Saturday defended their attacks on Iran, which called resulting civilian deaths a "war crime" during an emergency meeting of the UN Security Council. "The international community has long affirmed a simple and necessary principle: Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon," said US ambassador Mike Waltz, pointing to past UN resolutions ignored by Tehran. "That principle is not a matter of politics, it's a matter of global security, and to that end, the United States is taking lawful actions," he added.

title_original: Key military sites targeted inside Iran as part of coordinated US-Israeli strikes
content_original: In coordinated, sweeping U.S.–Israeli strikes on Iran in the predawn hours of Saturday, key military and nuclear-linked sites were targeted inside the country. The strikes focused on what U.S. officials described as high-value Iranian targets , which included Iran Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) command and control facilities, naval assets and underground sites believed to be associated with Iran’s nuclear program. In addition, Iranian air defense weapons, missile and drone launch sites, and military airfields were also targeted, according to officials. Israeli forces targeted sites linked to Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, a senior Israeli official confirmed to Fox News. President Donald Trump confirmed Saturday afternoon that Khamenei had been killed in a strike. He is among more than 40 senior Iranian security and regime figures killed in the attack, a senior Israeli official told Fox News. IF KHAMENEI FALLS, WHO TAKES IRAN? STRIKES WILL EXPOSE POWER VACUUM — AND THE ...

content_original: Israel struck its key target in Tehran Saturday in what a defense expert has described as a multi-million dollar precision-guided attack alongside a broader offensive involving U.S. waves of lower-cost kamikaze drones. Cameron Chell, CEO of drone manufacturer Draganfly , told Fox News Digital the campaign would have likely paired advanced and costly assets against Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s compound, while U.S. forces used cheaper drones to "overwhelm" on land, air and sea. "Saturday saw an overwhelming daytime attack with incredible intelligence to target the leadership and a strike on the compound possibly costing tens of millions," Chell said. WORLD LEADERS SPLIT OVER MILITARY ACTION AS US-ISRAEL STRIKE IRAN IN COORDINATED OPERATION "That would likely have included expensive, precision-strike drones or manned aircraft in highly coordinated attacks to ensure success, not necessarily the lower-cost, one-way version of the suicide drones," he explained. title_original: Netanyahu says Iran war to continue ‘as long as necessary’, claims Khamenei likely killed
content_original: Netanyahu says Iran war to continue ‘as long as necessary’, claims Khamenei likely killed Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has issued his first statement since launching the war on Iran. “This fateful operation will continue as long as necessary - patience is needed. This war will bring about true peace,” Netanyahu claimed on Saturday evening. He also appeared to reference reports of an attempt on the life of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. “We destroyed the compound of the dictator Khamenei. There are many signs that this dictator is gone,” he said. In a direct message to Iranians, Netanyahu urged public unrest. “To the citizens of Iran - do not miss the opportunity. Your moment will soon come to take to the streets, complete the task, and overthrow the regime that is making your lives miserable.”
title_en: Netanyahu says Iran war to continue ‘as long as necessary’, claims Khamenei likely killed

Medical sources told Anadolu that Abed Raed Radi was killed near Israeli-controlled areas east of the Zeitoun neighborhood. Salman Yahya al-Malalha, 16, was wounded in the same shelling and remains in moderate condition. Earlier in the day, two Palestinians were wounded when Israeli naval forces opened fire on fishing boats off Gaza City and in the Sheikh Ajlin area. A Palestinian woman was also shot in the foot in the Mawasi area west of Khan Younis, the sources added. Israel has killed 618 Palestinians and wounded 1,663 since a ceasefire […]

title_original: Israel's largest ever military flyover hammers Iranian military targets
content_original: Israel pummeled Iranian targets with simultaneous airstrikes from roughly 200 fighter jets in what the Israeli military dubbed its largest ever military flyover in its history. The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) on Saturday published video of its airstrike on missile launchers in western Iran as Israel and the U.S. take part in a joint effort against Tehran. The IDF said the fighter jets flew under the direction of IDF intelligence and the Israeli Air Force and executed an "extensive attack" against the Iranian regime's missile and defense systems in western and central Iran. "This is the largest military flyover in the history of the Israeli Air Force (IAF), conducted following accurate planning and based on high-quality intelligence, while synchronizing hundreds of fighter jets at the same time," the IDF said. TRUMP TELLS IRANIANS 'THE HOUR OF YOUR FREEDOM IS AT HAND' AS US-ISRAEL LAUNCH STRIKES AGAINST IRAN Fighter jets dropped hundreds of munitions on about 500 targets, including aeri...

summary_en: Israel conducted its largest-ever military flyover, with approximately 200 fighter jets striking missile launchers and defense systems in western and central Iran. The operation, directed by IDF intelligence and the Israeli Air Force, represents a significant escalation in joint Israeli-US efforts against Tehran. This extensive attack on Iranian military infrastructure highlights the deepening conflict between Israel and Iran.

classification.brief: Israel's largest-ever airstrike on Iranian military sites marks a significant escalation in the conflict, showcasing deepening Israeli-US cooperation against Tehran.

The strikes highlight ongoing tensions and potential for renewed conflict along Israel's northern border.

title_en: Iranian Shahed Drone targets US base in Qatar

title_original: Gulf states on frontlines of Israel-US war on Iran
content_original: Iran launched missile and drone strikes across multiple Arab states hosting US military bases on Saturday, hours after coordinated US-Israeli attacks on Iranian territory . The strikes threaten to widen the conflict to involve at least six countries across the Gulf and Levant , most of whom have long feared they would be on the front lines of any regional war. Air defences were activated in Qatar, the UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan, Iraq and Saudi Arabia as Iranian projectiles targeted US-linked military infrastructure across the region. At least one civilian was killed in Abu Dhabi when debris from intercepted missiles struck a residential area, according to Emirati authorities. The escalation followed large-scale unprovoked US and Israeli strikes on Iranian military and strategic targets earlier in the day, including in and around Tehran. US President Donald Trump described the campaign as "massive and ongoing combat operations" aimed at countering Iranian threats. Israeli officials fr...
title_en: Gulf states on frontlines of Israel-US war on Iran
summary_en: Iran launched missile and drone strikes targeting US military bases across multiple Arab states, including Qatar, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan, Iraq, and Saudi Arabia, following coordinated US-Israeli attacks on Iranian territory. The escalation threatens to widen the conflict, involving at least six countries in the Gulf and Levant. The activation of air defenses and the death of at least one civilian in Abu Dhabi highlight the direct impact on regional stability and civilian populations.

classification.location: Qatar, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan, Iraq, Saudi Arabia
classification.brief: Iran's missile and drone strikes on US military bases across multiple Arab states mark a significant escalation with direct engagement and regional instability.

title_original: Explosion at Dubai landmark The Palm: witnesses
content_original: Two witnesses told AFP they heard an explosion and saw a plume of smoke rising from Dubai's famed man-made island The Palm as authorities reported four injured. The attack came as Iran carried out retaliatory strikes in the oil-rich Gulf following US and Israeli attacks -- rattling a region long seen as a haven of peace and security. One of the witnesses said he saw thick black smoke rising from a hotel on the Palm and heard ambulances rushing towards the scene.
title_en: Explosion at Dubai landmark The Palm
summary_en: An explosion occurred at Dubai's Palm Jumeirah, injuring four people. This incident comes amid escalating tensions as Iran retaliates against US and Israeli attacks in the Gulf region. The attack highlights the potential for regional instability and the spillover of the Iran-US conflict into areas previously considered secure.

title_original: إيران ترد برشقات صاروخية على كيان الاحتلال
content_original: المركز الفلسطيني للإعلام بدأت إيران الرد على العدوان الأمريكي الإسرائيلي، بإطلاق موجة من الصواريخ الباليستية صوب أهداف في كيان الاحتلال. وأعلن الحرس الثوري الإيراني في بيان: ردًا على عدوان العدو المعتدي والقاتل على الجمهورية الإسلامية الإيرانية، بدأت أول موجة من الهجمات الصاروخية والطائرات المسيرة الواسعة للجمهورية الإسلامية الإيرانية نحو الأراضي المحتلة. وقالت مواقع عبرية، إن صفارات الإنذار دوت في جميع مدن إسرائيل مع صدور تعليمات بالدخول إلى الملاجئ، بعد إعلان حالة “طوارئ خاصة”. وأورد موقع والا الإسرائيلي أن أصوات الانفجارات سُمعت في منطقة حيفا، كما ذكرت وسائل إعلام إسرائيلية أخرى دوي انفجارات قوية في منطقة الجليل شمال إسرائيل. وتحدثث وسائل إعلام عبرية عن إسقاط صاروخ في سماء تل أبيب، وسقوط صاروخين في منطقة مفتوحة أخرى شمال فلسطين المحتلة. ولاحقا أقرت وسائل إعلام عبرية باستهدف مبنى مباشرة في عسقلان ومبنى في تل أبيب. وفي تطور لاحق، رصدت وسائل إعلام انطلاق موجة صواريخ إيرانية جديدة على كيان الاحتلال. الحرب على إيران .. هجمات إسرائيلية وأميركية على طهران وفي أول تعليق على الحر...
title_en: Iran launches ballistic missile barrage at Israel
summary_en: Iran's IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting Israel in response to US-Israeli aggression against Iran. Air raid sirens sounded across Israeli cities, with reports of explosions in Haifa and the Galilee region. This escalation signifies a significant expansion of the conflict with potential regional implications.

classification.brief: Iran's ballistic missile barrage against Israel marks a critical escalation, directly involving Iran in the conflict and posing significant regional security risks.

title_original: Lebanon refuses to be drawn into Iran conflict: PM
content_original: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam said Saturday that his country refused to be dragged into war, after Israel and the United States launched strikes against Iran. Authorities fear the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah could become involved in the conflict, which saw the US and Israel bomb Iran on Saturday and Tehran respond with missile attacks. Just before the operation began, Israel announced it was carrying out strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in south Lebanon.
title_en: Lebanon refuses to be drawn into Iran conflict: PM
summary_en: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam stated that Lebanon will not be drawn into the ongoing conflict after US and Israeli strikes on Iran, amid concerns that Hezbollah might get involved. Israel preemptively struck Hezbollah infrastructure in south Lebanon before the operation began. This highlights Lebanon's efforts to avoid escalation and the potential for regional spillover as the conflict intensifies.

classification.brief: Israel's preemptive strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in Lebanon signal potential escalation risks as Lebanon seeks to avoid involvement in the broader Iran conflict.

summary_en: The US and Israel have initiated a preventive attack on Iran, with Tehran's key centers, including government facilities, targeted. A state of emergency has been declared across Iran. The attack aims to prevent Iran's nuclear capabilities, with President Trump stating the objective is to ensure Iran does not acquire nuclear weapons.

classification.brief: The US and Israel launched a coordinated airstrike on Iran targeting government facilities and IRGC centers, marking a significant escalation in the conflict with mass casualties reported.

title_original: Syrian teen killed in Israeli airstrike in eastern Lebanon
content_original: A Syrian teen was killed and 29 others injured Thursday in Israeli airstrikes in eastern Lebanon despite a ceasefire deal that has been in place since November 2024, Lebanese state media reported, Anadolu reports. The National News Agency (NNA) said a 17-year-old boy was killed in the strike that targeted the Beit Mshik farms area near Kfar Dan in western Baalbek. The fatality came as Israeli warplanes launched a series of airstrikes targeting the western Baalbek area, with fighter jets flying at low altitude above the region. The Israeli army said the strikes targeted infrastructure belonging to the Lebanese group Hezbollah’s “Radwan Force” in the Baalbek area. In southern Lebanon, an Israeli drone carried out a strike with a guided […]
title_en: Syrian teen killed in Israeli airstrike in eastern Lebanon
summary_en: An Israeli airstrike in eastern Lebanon killed a Syrian teenager and injured 29 others near Kfar Dan in Baalbek. The strikes targeted Hezbollah's 'Radwan Force' infrastructure in the area, violating the existing ceasefire deal since November 2024. This incident highlights ongoing tensions and potential escalation between Israel and Hezbollah despite the ceasefire.

classification.brief: The Israeli airstrike targeting Hezbollah's 'Radwan Force' infrastructure in Lebanon, despite the ceasefire, signifies ongoing tensions and potential escalation in the region, indirectly impacting the Iran–US conflict through Hezbollah's role as an Iranian proxy.
